questions.explanation: Not knowing how long the wait will be triggers more anxiety than the wait itself — uncertainty, not duration, is the core stressor.

title: The Letter I Never Sent
text_zh: 搬家的时候，我在抽屉最里面发现了一封信。信已经写好了，信封也贴了邮票，但从来没有寄出去。那是三年前写给一个朋友的，当时我们因为一件小事吵了架，我想写信道歉。我记得那天晚上写了很久，反复修改每一句话，怕说得太多，也怕说得太少。写完以后我把信放在桌上，打算第二天去寄。但是第二天我犹豫了，觉得也许再等一等比较好。后来这封信就被其他东西盖住了，慢慢地忘记了。现在重新读一遍，发现当时的自己其实很真诚。也许不完美，但那些话是真心的。我拿出手机，给那个朋友发了一条消息：「好久不见，最近怎么样？」有些话，晚了三年也比永远不说要好。
text_pinyin: Bānjiā de shíhou, wǒ zài chōuti zuì lǐmiàn fāxiàn le yì fēng xìn. Xìn yǐjīng xiě hǎo le, xìnfēng yě tiē le yóupiào, dàn cónglái méiyǒu jì chūqù. Nà shì sān nián qián xiě gěi yí gè péngyou de, dāngshí wǒmen yīnwèi yí jiàn xiǎo shì chǎo le jià, wǒ xiǎng xiě xìn dàoqiàn. Wǒ jìde nà tiān wǎnshang xiě le hěn jiǔ, fǎnfù xiūgǎi měi yí jù huà, pà shuō de tài duō, yě pà shuō de tài shǎo. Xiě wán yǐhòu wǒ bǎ xìn fàng zài zhuō shàng, dǎsuàn dì èr tiān qù jì. Dànshì dì èr tiān wǒ yóuyù le, juéde yěxǔ zài děng yì děng bǐjiào hǎo. Hòulái zhè fēng xìn jiù bèi qítā dōngxi gài zhù le, mànmàn de wàngjì le. Xiànzài chóngxīn dú yí biàn, fāxiàn dāngshí de zìjǐ qíshí hěn zhēnchéng. Yěxǔ bù wánměi, dàn nàxiē huà shì zhēnxīn de. Wǒ ná chū shǒujī, gěi nàge péngyou fā le yì tiáo xiāoxi:「hǎo jiǔ bú jiàn, zuìjìn zěnmeyàng?」Yǒuxiē huà, wǎn le sān nián yě bǐ yǒngyuǎn bù shuō yào hǎo.
text_en: While moving, I found a letter in the back of a drawer. It was already written, the envelope stamped, but never sent. It was written three years ago to a friend — we had argued over something small and I wanted to write an apology. I remember writing for a long time that night, revising every sentence, afraid of saying too much or too little. After finishing I left it on the desk, planning to mail it the next day. But the next day I hesitated, thinking maybe it was better to wait. Eventually the letter was buried under other things and gradually forgotten. Reading it again now, I find that my past self was actually quite sincere. Maybe not perfect, but those words were heartfelt. I took out my phone and sent that friend a message: 'Long time no see, how have you been?' Some words, even three years late, are better than never said at all.

questions.q_zh: 这封信为什么没有寄出去？
questions.q_en: Why was the letter never sent?

options.text: 作者犹豫了，后来忘记了
options.pinyin: zuòzhě yóuyù le, hòulái wàngjì le

questions.explanation: The passage explains: eventually the letter was buried under other.

questions.q_zh: 最后作者做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the author finally do?
questions.explanation: After years of hesitation, the author finally sent a message -- better late than never.

title: Rain on the Window
text_zh: 下雨天的下午，我什么也不想做，只是坐在窗前看雨。雨滴落在玻璃上，一颗一颗慢慢往下滑，有时候两颗会合在一起，变成一颗更大的，然后滑得更快。小时候我总是选一颗雨滴，看它能不能「赢」——比别的雨滴先滑到窗户下面。现在我不再比赛了，只是安静地看着。窗外的树被雨洗得很绿，街上的人走得比平时快，伞的颜色在灰色的背景里特别明显。远处有人在收晾在外面的衣服，动作很急。我泡了一杯茶，听着雨声，觉得这样的下午其实很难得。平时总觉得应该做点什么，但下雨天给了我一个理由什么都不做。
text_pinyin: Xià yǔ tiān de xiàwǔ, wǒ shénme yě bù xiǎng zuò, zhǐ shì zuò zài chuāng qián kàn yǔ. Yǔdī luò zài bōli shàng, yì kē yì kē mànmàn wǎng xià huá, yǒu shíhou liǎng kē huì hé zài yìqǐ, biàn chéng yì kē gèng dà de, ránhòu huá de gèng kuài. Xiǎo shíhou wǒ zǒng shì xuǎn yì kē yǔdī, kàn tā néng bù néng「yíng」—— bǐ bié de yǔdī xiān huá dào chuānghu xiàmiàn. Xiànzài wǒ bú zài bǐsài le, zhǐ shì ānjìng de kàn zhe. Chuāng wài de shù bèi yǔ xǐ de hěn lǜ, jiē shàng de rén zǒu de bǐ píngshí kuài, sǎn de yánsè zài huīsè de bèijǐng lǐ tèbié míngxiǎn. Yuǎn chù yǒu rén zài shōu liàng zài wàimiàn de yīfu, dòngzuò hěn jí. Wǒ pào le yì bēi chá, tīng zhe yǔ shēng, juéde zhèyàng de xiàwǔ qíshí hěn nándé. Píngshí zǒng juéde yīnggāi zuò diǎn shénme, dàn xià yǔ tiān gěi le wǒ yí gè lǐyóu shénme dōu bú zuò. Yěxǔ wǒmen dōu xūyào zhèyàng de shíkè, yǔnxǔ zìjǐ tíng xiàlái, zhǐ shì kàn kàn chuāng wài.
text_en: On a rainy afternoon, I don't want to do anything, just sit by the window watching the rain. Raindrops land on the glass, sliding down one by one, sometimes two merging into a bigger one that slides faster. As a child I would always pick a raindrop to see if it could 'win' — reach the bottom before the others. Now I don't race them anymore, just watch quietly. The trees outside are washed green by rain, people on the street walk faster than usual, umbrella colors stand out against the gray background. In the distance someone hurriedly gathers clothes drying outside. I steep a cup of tea, listening to the rain, thinking this kind of afternoon is actually rare. Usually I feel I should be doing something, but rainy days give me a reason to do nothing at all.

questions.q_zh: 小时候作者看雨的时候喜欢做什么？
questions.q_en: What did the author like to do while watching rain as a child?

options.text: 选一颗雨滴看它能不能先滑到底
options.pinyin: xuǎn yì kē yǔdī kàn tā néng bù néng xiān huá dào dǐ
options.text_en: Pick a raindrop to see if it slides down first
Draw the raindrops on the window

questions.explanation: As a child watching rain on the window, the author would pick a raindrop and race it against others.

questions.q_zh: 人物的行为暗示了什么内心想法？
questions.q_en: What inner thoughts does the character's behavior hint at?
questions.explanation: The deliberate, quiet actions reveal an underlying search for calm and purpose.

title: The Notebook on the Shelf
text_zh: 整理书架的时候，我翻到了大学时用的一本笔记本。封面已经旧了，角上有咖啡的痕迹。打开一看，里面记满了各种东西：上课的笔记、考试前画的重点、还有一些随手写下的想法。有一页写着「以后要做的事」，下面列了十件，比如学吉他、去西藏旅行、写一本小说。我数了一下，十年过去了，只完成了两件。但让我意外的是，我并不觉得难过。那些没有完成的事并不代表失败，它们只是说明生活把我带到了不同的方向。我没有学吉他，但我学会了做饭。我没有去西藏，但我去了很多当时没有想到的地方。也许人生不需要按照计划走，重要的是你一直在走。我把笔记本放回书架，觉得它属于那个时候的自己，而现在的自己也很好。
text_pinyin: Zhěnglǐ shūjià de shíhou, wǒ fān dào le dàxué shí yòng de yì běn bǐjìběn. Fēngmiàn yǐjīng jiù le, jiǎo shàng yǒu kāfēi de hénjì. Dǎkāi yí kàn, lǐmiàn jì mǎn le gè zhǒng dōngxi: shàngkè de bǐjì, kǎoshì qián huà de zhòngdiǎn, hái yǒu yìxiē suíshǒu xiě xià de xiǎngfǎ. Yǒu yí yè xiě zhe「yǐhòu yào zuò de shì」, xiàmiàn liè le shí jiàn, bǐrú xué jítā, qù Xīzàng lǚxíng, xiě yì běn xiǎoshuō. Wǒ shǔ le yíxià, shí nián guòqù le, zhǐ wánchéng le liǎng jiàn. Dàn ràng wǒ yìwài de shì, wǒ bìng bù juéde nánguò. Nàxiē méiyǒu wánchéng de shì bìng bú dàibiǎo shībài, tāmen zhǐ shì shuōmíng shēnghuó bǎ wǒ dài dào le bùtóng de fāngxiàng. Wǒ méiyǒu xué jítā, dàn wǒ xuéhuì le zuòfàn. Wǒ méiyǒu qù Xīzàng, dàn wǒ qù le hěn duō dāngshí méiyǒu xiǎng dào de dìfāng. Yěxǔ rénshēng bù xūyào ànzhào jìhuà zǒu, zhòngyào de shì nǐ yìzhí zài zǒu. Wǒ bǎ bǐjìběn fàng huí shūjià, juéde tā shǔyú nàge shíhou de zìjǐ, ér xiànzài de zìjǐ yě hěn hǎo.
text_en: While organizing my bookshelf, I came across a notebook from college. The cover was worn, with coffee stains on the corners. Inside it was filled with all sorts of things: class notes, exam highlights, and some thoughts jotted down casually. One page read 'Things to do in the future' with ten items listed — learn guitar, travel to Tibet, write a novel. I counted: ten years later, only two were completed. But surprisingly, I didn't feel sad. Those unfinished things don't represent failure; they just show that life took me in different directions. I didn't learn guitar, but I learned to cook. I didn't go to Tibet, but I went to many places I hadn't imagined back then. Maybe life doesn't need to follow a plan — what matters is that you keep going. I put the notebook back on the shelf, feeling it belongs to who I was then, and who I am now is also fine.

questions.q_zh: 如果另一个人来到同样的场景，会有同样的感受吗？
questions.q_en: Would another person in the same setting have the same feelings?

options.text: 不一定，取决于个人经历
options.pinyin: bù yídìng, qǔjué yú gèrén jīnglì
options.text_en: not necessarily, it depends on personal experience

questions.explanation: Subjective responses depend on individual history -- same scene, different emotional resonance.

questions.q_zh: 如果把这段话和新闻报道比较，最大的区别是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the biggest difference between this passage and a news report?
questions.explanation: A news report would give who/what/when; this passage prioritizes emotional texture instead.

title: The Morning Market
text_zh: 周末早上六点半，我去了一趟早市。太阳刚刚升起来，空气还有点凉。摊位已经摆好了，蔬菜上还带着露水，看起来特别新鲜。卖豆腐的大叔把白白的豆腐整齐地摆在案板上，旁边放着一把切豆腐的刀。卖水果的阿姨一边整理橘子一边和旁边卖鸡蛋的人聊天。我买了一些青菜和两个西红柿，阿姨多给了我一根葱，说「回去做汤放一点」。早市的声音很杂，但不让人觉得吵。有人在讨价还价，有人在叫卖，有人推着小推车慢慢走。这些场景让我想起小时候跟着奶奶赶早市，她总是能用最少的钱买到最好的菜。虽然现在超市什么都有，但早市有一种温度，是超市里感受不到的。
text_pinyin: Zhōumò zǎoshang liù diǎn bàn, wǒ qù le yí tàng zǎo shì. Tàiyáng gānggāng shēng qǐlái, kōngqì hái yǒudiǎn liáng. Tānwèi yǐjīng bǎi hǎo le, shūcài shàng hái dài zhe lùshuǐ, kàn qǐlái tèbié xīnxiān. Mài dòufu de dàshū bǎ bái bái de dòufu zhěngqí de bǎi zài ànbǎn shàng, pángbiān fàng zhe yì bǎ qiē dòufu de dāo. Mài shuǐguǒ de āyí yìbiān zhěnglǐ júzi yìbiān hé pángbiān mài jīdàn de rén liáo tiān. Wǒ mǎi le yìxiē qīngcài hé liǎng gè xīhóngshì, āyí duō gěi le wǒ yì gēn cōng, shuō「huíqù zuò tāng fàng yìdiǎn」. Zǎo shì de shēngyīn hěn zá, dàn bú ràng rén juéde chǎo. Yǒu rén zài tǎojià huánjià, yǒu rén zài jiào mài, yǒu rén tuī zhe xiǎo tuī chē mànmàn zǒu. Zhèxiē chǎngjǐng ràng wǒ xiǎngqǐ xiǎo shíhou gēn zhe nǎinai gǎn zǎo shì, tā zǒng shì néng yòng zuì shǎo de qián mǎi dào zuì hǎo de cài. Suīrán xiànzài chāoshì shénme dōu yǒu, dàn zǎo shì yǒu yì zhǒng wēndù, shì chāoshì lǐ gǎnshòu bú dào de.
text_en: At 6:30 on a weekend morning, I went to the morning market. The sun had just risen, the air still a bit cool. Stalls were already set up, vegetables still dewy, looking especially fresh. The tofu uncle arranged white blocks neatly on his board, a cutting knife beside them. The fruit auntie organized oranges while chatting with the egg seller next to her. I bought some greens and two tomatoes; the auntie threw in an extra scallion, saying 'put some in your soup when you get home.' The morning market sounds are mixed but not annoying. Some people bargain, some hawk their goods, some push small carts slowly. These scenes remind me of going to the morning market with grandma as a child — she could always buy the best vegetables with the least money. Though supermarkets now have everything, the morning market has a warmth you can't feel in a supermarket.

questions.q_zh: 阿姨多给了作者什么？
questions.q_en: What extra did the auntie give the author?
questions.explanation: After buying greens and tomatoes, the vendor tossed in a free scallion — a customary market gesture of goodwill toward a regular buyer.

questions.q_zh: 为什么这段话值得仔细读第二遍？
questions.q_en: Why is this passage worth reading a second time?

options.text: 能发现更多隐含的意义
options.pinyin: néng fāxiàn gèng duō yǐnhán de yìyì
options.text_en: you can discover more hidden meanings

options.text: 内容太复杂，一遍读不懂
options.pinyin: nèiróng tài fùzá, yí biàn dú bù dǒng
options.text_en: it's too complex to understand in one reading

options.pinyin: bù zhíde dú dì èr biàn

questions.explanation: Layered details reward re-reading -- subtext emerges that a first pass may miss.

title: Learning to Say No
text_zh: 以前我很难拒绝别人。同事请我帮忙，即使自己已经很忙了，我还是会说好。朋友约我出去，即使很累了，我也不好意思说不去。时间长了，我发现自己总是在满足别人的需要，却忘了自己的感受。有一次我答应帮同事做一个方案，结果连续加班三天，自己的工作也耽误了，身体也很不舒服。那天晚上我认真想了想，觉得不能再这样了。我不是不愿意帮忙，而是应该先照顾好自己。后来再有人找我帮忙的时候，如果我真的没有时间，我会诚实地说「对不起，这次我帮不了」。刚开始说的时候心里有点不安，但慢慢发现大家其实都能理解。学会说不并不是变得自私，而是学会尊重自己的时间和精力。
text_pinyin: Yǐqián wǒ hěn nán jùjué biéren. Tóngshì qǐng wǒ bāngmáng, jíshǐ zìjǐ yǐjīng hěn máng le, wǒ háishi huì shuō hǎo. Péngyou yuē wǒ chūqù, jíshǐ hěn lèi le, wǒ yě bù hǎoyìsi shuō bú qù. Shíjiān cháng le, wǒ fāxiàn zìjǐ zǒng shì zài mǎnzú biéren de xūyào, què wàng le zìjǐ de gǎnshòu. Yǒu yí cì wǒ dāying bāng tóngshì zuò yí gè fāng'àn, jiéguǒ liánxù jiābān sān tiān, zìjǐ de gōngzuò yě dānwu le, shēntǐ yě hěn bù shūfu. Nà tiān wǎnshang wǒ rènzhēn xiǎng le xiǎng, juéde bù néng zài zhèyàng le. Wǒ bú shì bú yuànyì bāngmáng, ér shì yīnggāi xiān zhàogù hǎo zìjǐ. Hòulái zài yǒu rén zhǎo wǒ bāngmáng de shíhou, rúguǒ wǒ zhēn de méiyǒu shíjiān, wǒ huì chéngshí de shuō「duìbuqǐ, zhè cì wǒ bāng bù liǎo」. Gāng kāishǐ shuō de shíhou xīnlǐ yǒudiǎn bù ān, dàn mànmàn fāxiàn dàjiā qíshí dōu néng lǐjiě. Xuéhuì shuō bù bìng bú shì biàn de zìsī, ér shì xuéhuì zūnzhòng zìjǐ de shíjiān hé jīnglì.
text_en: I used to have a hard time refusing people. When colleagues asked for help, even if I was already busy, I'd still say yes. When friends invited me out, even if I was exhausted, I felt too embarrassed to decline. Over time, I realized I was always meeting others' needs while forgetting my own feelings. Once I agreed to help a colleague with a proposal and ended up working overtime three days straight — my own work fell behind and I felt terrible physically. That night I thought seriously about it and decided this couldn't continue. It's not that I'm unwilling to help, but I should take care of myself first. After that, when someone asked for help and I truly had no time, I'd honestly say 'Sorry, I can't help this time.' At first it felt uneasy, but gradually I found everyone actually understood. Learning to say no isn't becoming selfish — it's learning to respect your own time and energy.

questions.q_zh: 帮同事做方案以后发生了什么？
questions.q_en: What happened after helping the colleague with the proposal?

options.text: 加班三天，自己的工作耽误了
options.pinyin: jiābān sān tiān, zìjǐ de gōngzuò dānwu le
options.text_en: Worked overtime three days, own work delayed

questions.explanation: Cause-effect: after helping a colleague with a proposal, the author worked overtime three days and fell behind.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中什么是'看不见'但能感觉到的？
questions.q_en: What in this passage is 'invisible' but can be felt?

options.text: 人物的情感和内心世界
options.pinyin: rénwù de qínggǎn hé nèixīn shìjiè
options.text_en: the character's emotions and inner world

questions.explanation: No feelings are named outright, yet the actions and details radiate emotional undercurrent.

title: The Old Photograph
text_zh: 手机相册里存了几千张照片，但我最喜欢的一张是印出来的，贴在冰箱上。那是十年前拍的，一家人围着桌子吃火锅。照片有点模糊，颜色也不太准，但每个人都在笑。爷爷坐在最里面，正在往锅里放肉片。妈妈端着一碗芝麻酱，嘴巴张着好像在说什么。爸爸拿着筷子，夹了一块豆腐正要放进嘴里。我坐在旁边，低着头在看手机——那时候刚买了第一部智能手机。现在看这张照片才发现，那个低头看手机的自己错过了多少。爷爷三年前去世了，那样的家庭火锅再也没有过。我现在吃火锅的时候会把手机放在一边，认真看看对面的人，听他们说话。有些时刻过了就不会再来，而照片只能留住画面，留不住那天的温度和味道。
text_pinyin: Shǒujī xiàngcè lǐ cún le jǐ qiān zhāng zhàopiàn, dàn wǒ zuì xǐhuan de yì zhāng shì yìn chūlái de, tiē zài bīngxiāng shàng. Nà shì shí nián qián pāi de, yì jiā rén wéi zhe zhuōzi chī huǒguō. Zhàopiàn yǒudiǎn móhu, yánsè yě bú tài zhǔn, dàn měi gè rén dōu zài xiào. Yéye zuò zài zuì lǐmiàn, zhèngzài wǎng guō lǐ fàng ròu piàn. Māma duān zhe yì wǎn zhīmajiàng, zuǐba zhāng zhe hǎoxiàng zài shuō shénme. Bàba ná zhe kuàizi, jiā le yí kuài dòufu zhèng yào fàng jìn zuǐ lǐ. Wǒ zuò zài pángbiān, dī zhe tóu zài kàn shǒujī — nà shíhou gāng mǎi le dì yī bù zhìnéng shǒujī. Xiànzài kàn zhè zhāng zhàopiàn cái fāxiàn, nàge dī tóu kàn shǒujī de zìjǐ cuòguò le duōshǎo. Yéye sān nián qián qùshì le, nàyàng de jiātíng huǒguō zài yě méiyǒu guò. Wǒ xiànzài chī huǒguō de shíhou huì bǎ shǒujī fàng zài yìbiān, rènzhēn kàn kàn duìmiàn de rén, tīng tāmen shuōhuà. Yǒuxiē shíkè guò le jiù bú huì zài lái, ér zhàopiàn zhǐ néng liú zhù huàmiàn, liú bú zhù nà tiān de wēndù hé wèidào.
text_en: My phone album stores thousands of photos, but my favorite is a printed one stuck on the fridge. Taken ten years ago, the whole family sitting around a table eating hotpot. The photo is a bit blurry, the colors not quite right, but everyone is smiling. Grandpa sits in the innermost spot, putting meat slices into the pot. Mom holds a bowl of sesame paste, mouth open as if saying something. Dad holds chopsticks, a piece of tofu about to enter his mouth. I sit beside them, head down looking at my phone — I had just bought my first smartphone. Looking at this photo now, I realize how much that head-down, phone-watching self missed. Grandpa passed away three years ago; that kind of family hotpot never happened again. Now when I eat hotpot I put my phone aside and really look at the people across from me, listen to them talk. Some moments never come back once they pass, and photos can only preserve the image, not the warmth and taste of that day.

questions.q_zh: 照片里作者在做什么？
questions.q_en: What was the author doing in the photo?
questions.explanation: Looking back at the photo years later, the author sees themselves staring down at a phone instead of engaging with grandpa — a moment of regret captured in the image.

questions.q_zh: 作者现在吃火锅的时候有什么变化？
questions.q_en: How has the author changed when eating hotpot now?

options.text: 把手机放在一边，认真看对面的人
options.pinyin: bǎ shǒujī fàng zài yìbiān, rènzhēn kàn duìmiàn de rén
options.text_en: Puts phone aside, really looks at people across the table

options.pinyin: měi cì dōu pāi hěn duō zhàopiàn
options.text_en: Takes lots of photos every time

questions.explanation: Now the author puts the phone aside during hotpot and really looks at the people across the table.

title: Walking Without a Destination
text_zh: 昨天晚饭以后，我出门散步，没有计划去哪里。走出小区大门往左拐，沿着一条我平时不怎么走的路。路两边种着梧桐树，树叶在路灯下面看起来是浅黄色的。我走得很慢，注意到了很多平时没有注意过的东西：一家面包店的灯还亮着，里面有人在准备明天早上的面包；一只花猫坐在围墙上，用很认真的表情看着我走过去；一对老夫妻在公园长椅上坐着聊天，身边放着两杯茶。没有目的地的时候反而看到了更多。平时走路总是匆匆忙忙，从A点到B点，眼睛看着前方，脑子想着接下来的事。但昨天晚上什么都不想，只是走路，只是看。回到家的时候觉得心情比出门前好了很多。
text_pinyin: Zuótiān wǎnfàn yǐhòu, wǒ chūmén sànbù, méiyǒu jìhuà qù nǎlǐ. Zǒu chū xiǎoqū dàmén wǎng zuǒ guǎi, yán zhe yì tiáo wǒ píngshí bù zěnme zǒu de lù. Lù liǎng biān zhòng zhe wútóng shù, shùyè zài lùdēng xiàmiàn kàn qǐlái shì qiǎn huángsè de. Wǒ zǒu de hěn màn, zhùyì dào le hěn duō píngshí méiyǒu zhùyì guò de dōngxi: yì jiā miànbāo diàn de dēng hái liàng zhe, lǐmiàn yǒu rén zài zhǔnbèi míngtiān zǎoshang de miànbāo; yì zhī huā māo zuò zài wéiqiáng shàng, yòng hěn rènzhēn de biǎoqíng kàn zhe wǒ zǒu guòqù; yí duì lǎo fūqī zài gōngyuán cháng yǐ shàng zuò zhe liáo tiān, shēnbiān fàng zhe liǎng bēi chá. Méiyǒu mùdìdì de shíhou fǎn'ér kàn dào le gèng duō. Píngshí zǒulù zǒng shì cōngcōng mángmáng, cóng A diǎn dào B diǎn, yǎnjīng kàn zhe qiánfāng, nǎozi xiǎng zhe jiē xiàlái de shì. Dàn zuótiān wǎnshang shénme dōu bù xiǎng, zhǐ shì zǒulù, zhǐ shì kàn. Huí dào jiā de shíhou juéde xīnqíng bǐ chūmén qián hǎo le hěn duō. Yěxǔ sànbù zuì hǎo de zhuàngtài jiù shì méiyǒu mùdìdì, ràng jiǎobù dài zhe nǐ, ér bú shì nǐ dài zhe jiǎobù.
text_en: After dinner yesterday, I went for a walk with no plan of where to go. I left the compound gate and turned left, along a road I don't usually take. Sycamore trees lined both sides, their leaves looking pale yellow under the streetlights. I walked slowly and noticed many things I'd never noticed before: a bakery still lit up, someone inside preparing tomorrow morning's bread; a calico cat sitting on a wall, watching me pass with a very serious expression; an elderly couple chatting on a park bench, two cups of tea beside them. Without a destination, I actually saw more. Usually I walk hurriedly from point A to point B, eyes forward, mind on what's next. But last night I thought of nothing, just walked, just looked. When I got home my mood was much better than when I'd left.

questions.q_zh: 散步的时候作者看到了什么动物？
questions.q_en: What animal did the author see during the walk?
questions.explanation: During the aimless walk, the author spotted a calico cat sitting calmly on a wall.

questions.q_zh: 人物的行为暗示了什么内心想法？
questions.q_en: What inner thoughts does the character's behavior hint at?
questions.explanation: The deliberate, quiet actions reveal an underlying search for calm and purpose.

title: The Sound of Cooking
text_zh: 我发现做饭最让我放松的不是吃的那一刻，而是做的过程中那些声音。洗菜的水声，刀切在案板上的节奏，油在锅里发出的滋滋声，盖上锅盖以后小火慢炖的咕嘟咕嘟声。这些声音组合在一起，比任何音乐都让我觉得安心。有时候我会故意把手机放在客厅，不听播客也不放歌，就让厨房里只有做饭的声音。蒜瓣拍碎的声音很脆，西红柿下锅以后嗞啦一下很痛快。等到菜在锅里慢慢变熟，声音也变小了，变得温柔。我觉得这些声音之所以让人放松，是因为它们代表着一种确定性——你知道接下来会发生什么，你知道结果是什么。在一个什么都不确定的世界里，做饭是少数你能完全掌控的事情之一。从生的到熟的，从食材到一道菜，这个过程简单、可靠，让人踏实。
text_pinyin: Wǒ fāxiàn zuòfàn zuì ràng wǒ fàngsōng de bú shì chī de nà yí kè, ér shì zuò de guòchéng zhōng nàxiē shēngyīn. Xǐ cài de shuǐ shēng, dāo qiē zài ànbǎn shàng de jiézòu, yóu zài guō lǐ fāchū de zīzī shēng, gài shàng guō gài yǐhòu xiǎo huǒ màn dùn de gūdū gūdū shēng. Zhèxiē shēngyīn zǔhé zài yìqǐ, bǐ rènhé yīnyuè dōu ràng wǒ juéde ānxīn. Yǒu shíhou wǒ huì gùyì bǎ shǒujī fàng zài kètīng, bù tīng bōkè yě bú fàng gē, jiù ràng chúfáng lǐ zhǐ yǒu zuòfàn de shēngyīn. Suàn bàn pāi suì de shēngyīn hěn cuì, xīhóngshì xià guō yǐhòu zīlā yíxià hěn tòngkuai. Děng dào cài zài guō lǐ mànmàn biàn shú, shēngyīn yě biàn xiǎo le, biàn de wēnróu. Wǒ juéde zhèxiē shēngyīn zhī suǒyǐ ràng rén fàngsōng, shì yīnwèi tāmen dàibiǎo zhe yì zhǒng quèdìngxìng — nǐ zhīdào jiē xiàlái huì fāshēng shénme, nǐ zhīdào jiéguǒ shì shénme. Zài yí gè shénme dōu bú quèdìng de shìjiè lǐ, zuòfàn shì shǎoshù nǐ néng wánquán zhǎngkòng de shìqíng zhī yī. Cóng shēng de dào shú de, cóng shícái dào yí dào cài, zhège guòchéng jiǎndān, kěkào, ràng rén tāshi.
text_en: I've found that the most relaxing part of cooking isn't the moment of eating, but the sounds during the process. The water washing vegetables, the rhythm of the knife on the cutting board, the sizzle of oil in the wok, the bubbling after covering the lid to simmer on low heat. These sounds together make me feel more at ease than any music. Sometimes I deliberately leave my phone in the living room, no podcasts, no songs, just the sounds of cooking in the kitchen. The crisp crack of crushing a garlic clove, the satisfying sizzle when tomatoes hit the hot pan. As the food slowly cooks through, the sounds quiet down too, becoming gentle. I think these sounds are relaxing because they represent a kind of certainty — you know what will happen next, you know what the result will be. In a world where nothing is certain, cooking is one of the few things you can fully control. From raw to cooked, from ingredients to a finished dish — the process is simple, reliable, and grounding.

questions.q_zh: 做饭的时候作者为什么不听音乐？
questions.q_en: Why doesn't the author listen to music while cooking?

options.pinyin: xiǎng zhǐ tīng zuòfàn de shēngyīn
options.text_en: Wants to hear only cooking sounds

questions.explanation: The passage explains: but the sounds during the process.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么觉得做饭让人放松？
questions.q_en: Why does the author find cooking relaxing?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi néng chī dào hǎo chī de
options.text_en: Because you get to eat good food

options.text: 因为做饭代表一种确定性，能完全掌控
options.pinyin: yīnwèi zuòfàn dàibiǎo yì zhǒng quèdìngxìng, néng wánquán zhǎngkòng
options.text_en: Because cooking represents certainty and full control

options.pinyin: yīnwèi bú yòng hé biéren shuōhuà
options.text_en: Because you don't need to talk to anyone

questions.explanation: The passage explains: in a world where nothing is certain.

title: The Plant on My Desk
text_zh: 半年前同事送了我一盆小绿植，说放在办公桌上可以缓解眼睛疲劳。我收下了，但其实并没有太在意。浇水常常忘记，有一次出差一个星期回来发现叶子都蔫了。我赶紧浇了水，第二天它居然又恢复了精神。从那以后我开始认真照顾它，每天早上到办公室的第一件事就是看看它需不需要浇水。慢慢地我发现它长出了新叶子，颜色比旧叶子浅一些，嫩嫩的。有一天阳光特别好，我把它搬到窗台上晒太阳，下午的时候它的叶子好像比早上更绿了。照顾一棵植物的过程教会了我耐心。它不会因为你着急就长得快一点，也不会因为你忽略它就马上死掉。它有自己的节奏，你只需要持续地给它需要的东西。
text_pinyin: Bàn nián qián tóngshì sòng le wǒ yì pén xiǎo lǜzhí, shuō fàng zài bàngōngzhuō shàng kěyǐ huǎnjiě yǎnjīng píláo. Wǒ shōu xià le, dàn qíshí bìng méiyǒu tài zàiyì. Jiāo shuǐ chángcháng wàngjì, yǒu yí cì chūchāi yí gè xīngqī huílái fāxiàn yèzi dōu niān le. Wǒ gǎnjǐn jiāo le shuǐ, dì èr tiān tā jūrán yòu huīfù le jīngshén. Cóng nà yǐhòu wǒ kāishǐ rènzhēn zhàogù tā, měi tiān zǎoshang dào bàngōngshì de dì yī jiàn shì jiù shì kàn kàn tā xū bù xūyào jiāo shuǐ. Mànmàn de wǒ fāxiàn tā zhǎng chū le xīn yèzi, yánsè bǐ jiù yèzi qiǎn yìxiē, nèn nèn de. Yǒu yì tiān yángguāng tèbié hǎo, wǒ bǎ tā bān dào chuāngtái shàng shài tàiyáng, xiàwǔ de shíhou tā de yèzi hǎoxiàng bǐ zǎoshang gèng lǜ le. Zhàogù yì kē zhíwù de guòchéng jiāo huì le wǒ nàixīn. Tā bú huì yīnwèi nǐ zháojí jiù zhǎng de kuài yìdiǎn, yě bú huì yīnwèi nǐ hūlüè tā jiù mǎshàng sǐ diào. Tā yǒu zìjǐ de jiézòu, nǐ zhǐ xūyào chíxù de gěi tā xūyào de dōngxi. Yěxǔ duìdài zìjǐ de chéngzhǎng, yě yīnggāi xiàng duìdài yì kē zhíwù yíyàng yǒu nàixīn.
text_en: Six months ago a colleague gave me a small plant, saying putting it on my desk would ease eye strain. I accepted it but didn't really care. I often forgot to water it, and once after a week-long business trip I returned to find its leaves all wilted. I quickly watered it, and the next day it had surprisingly perked back up. After that I started caring for it properly — the first thing each morning at the office was checking if it needed water. Gradually I noticed it grew new leaves, slightly lighter than the old ones, soft and tender. One day when the sun was especially good, I moved it to the windowsill to bask, and by afternoon its leaves seemed greener than in the morning. The process of caring for a plant taught me patience. It won't grow faster because you're anxious, and it won't die immediately because you neglect it. It has its own rhythm; you just need to consistently give it what it needs.

questions.q_zh: 出差回来以后绿植怎么了？
questions.q_en: What happened to the plant after the business trip?

options.pinyin: zhǎng le hěn duō xīn yèzi

options.text_en: Was cared for by a colleague

questions.explanation: After a week-long business trip, the author returned to find all the plant's leaves wilted.

questions.q_zh: 照顾植物教会了作者什么？
questions.q_en: What did caring for the plant teach the author?
questions.explanation: Watching a plant grow slowly day by day, with no way to rush it, taught the author patience — growth happens on its own schedule.

title: A Quiet Sunday
text_zh: 周日早上醒来已经九点了，窗帘缝里透进来一条阳光。我没有马上起床，躺在被子里听外面的声音。楼下有人在遛狗，远处有小孩在笑，偶尔传来一声鸟叫。今天没有任何安排，这种感觉很久没有过了。起床以后我煮了咖啡，把早餐摆在阳台的小桌上，一边吃一边看楼下的人来人往。下午我翻了一本放了很久没看完的书，看了大概五十页。有时候看着看着走神了，就放下书发一会儿呆，然后再接着看。傍晚的时候我去楼下散了一圈步，买了一个西瓜回来。晚上看了一部老电影，泡了脚，十点就睡了。一天下来什么特别的事都没做，但我觉得这是最近最充实的一天。
text_pinyin: Zhōurì zǎoshang xǐng lái yǐjīng jiǔ diǎn le, chuānglián fèng lǐ tòu jìnlái yì tiáo yángguāng. Wǒ méiyǒu mǎshàng qǐchuáng, tǎng zài bèizi lǐ tīng wàimiàn de shēngyīn. Lóu xià yǒu rén zài liú gǒu, yuǎn chù yǒu xiǎohái zài xiào, ǒu'ěr chuán lái yì shēng niǎo jiào. Jīntiān méiyǒu rènhé ānpái, zhè zhǒng gǎnjué hěn jiǔ méiyǒu guò le. Qǐchuáng yǐhòu wǒ zhǔ le kāfēi, bǎ zǎocān bǎi zài yángtái de xiǎo zhuō shàng, yìbiān chī yìbiān kàn lóu xià de rén lái rén wǎng. Xiàwǔ wǒ fān le yì běn fàng le hěn jiǔ méi kàn wán de shū, kàn le dàgài wǔshí yè. Yǒu shíhou kàn zhe kàn zhe zǒu shén le, jiù fàng xià shū fā yíhuìr dāi, ránhòu zài jiē zhe kàn. Bàngwǎn de shíhou wǒ qù lóu xià sàn le yì quān bù, mǎi le yí gè xīguā huílái. Wǎnshang kàn le yí bù lǎo diànyǐng, pào le jiǎo, shí diǎn jiù shuì le. Yì tiān xiàlái shénme tèbié de shì dōu méi zuò, dàn wǒ juéde zhè shì zuìjìn zuì chōngshí de yì tiān. Yěxǔ wǒmen yìzhí zài zhuīqiú「yǒu yìyì」de shēnghuó, què hūlüè le「méiyǒu yìyì」de rìzi běnshēn jiù shì yì zhǒng yìyì.
text_en: Woke up Sunday morning at nine already, a sliver of sunlight slipping through the curtain gap. I didn't get up right away, just lay under the covers listening to sounds outside. Someone downstairs walking a dog, children laughing in the distance, an occasional bird call. No plans today — a feeling I haven't had in a long time. After getting up I brewed coffee, set breakfast on the small balcony table, eating while watching people come and go below. In the afternoon I picked up a book I'd left unfinished for a while, read about fifty pages. Sometimes my mind wandered and I'd put the book down and zone out, then pick it up again. In the evening I took a lap downstairs, bought a watermelon on the way back. At night I watched an old movie, soaked my feet, and was asleep by ten. Nothing special happened all day, but I felt it was the most fulfilling day in recent memory.

questions.q_zh: 下午作者做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did the author do in the afternoon?
questions.explanation: The author spent the afternoon reading about fifty pages of a book, a quiet activity that filled the day without agenda.

questions.q_zh: 作者对这一天的感受是什么？
questions.q_en: How did the author feel about this day?

options.text: 觉得是最近最充实的一天
options.pinyin: juéde shì zuìjìn zuì chōngshí de yì tiān
options.text_en: Felt it was the most fulfilling day recently

options.pinyin: juéde yīnggāi duō zuò yìxiē shì

questions.explanation: Nothing special happened all day, yet the author felt it was the most fulfilling day recently -- a paradox.

title: The Old Jacket
text_zh: 换季的时候整理衣柜，拿出了一件旧外套。它已经洗得有点褪色了，右边的口袋里居然还有一张电影票。日期是四年前的冬天，电影名字我已经不记得了，但我记得那天的事。那是和一个很久没见的老朋友看的。我们高中毕业以后各自去了不同的城市，平时很少联系。那年冬天他来出差，约我出来见个面。我们看完电影以后去了一家小饭馆，点了啤酒和花生米，聊了三个多小时。没有聊什么深刻的话题，就是说说各自的近况，回忆了一些上学时候的事情，笑了很多。分开的时候他说「别太久不联系了」，我说「好」。但后来我们又很久没联系了。我把电影票从口袋里拿出来，夹在书里。旧外套可以扔掉，但这张票我想留着。它不只是一张票，它是一个证据——证明那天晚上我们确实很开心。
text_pinyin: Huàn jì de shíhou zhěnglǐ yīguì, ná chū le yí jiàn jiù wàitào. Tā yǐjīng xǐ de yǒudiǎn tuìsè le, yòubiān de kǒudài lǐ jūrán hái yǒu yì zhāng diànyǐng piào. Rìqī shì sì nián qián de dōngtiān, diànyǐng míngzi wǒ yǐjīng bú jìde le, dàn wǒ jìde nà tiān de shì. Nà shì hé yí gè hěn jiǔ méi jiàn de lǎo péngyou kàn de. Wǒmen gāozhōng bìyè yǐhòu gèzì qù le bùtóng de chéngshì, píngshí hěn shǎo liánxì. Nà nián dōngtiān tā lái chūchāi, yuē wǒ chūlái jiàn gè miàn. Wǒmen kàn wán diànyǐng yǐhòu qù le yì jiā xiǎo fànguǎn, diǎn le píjiǔ hé huāshēng mǐ, liáo le sān gè duō xiǎoshí. Méiyǒu liáo shénme shēnkè de huàtí, jiù shì shuō shuō gèzì de jìnkuàng, huíyì le yìxiē shàngxué shíhou de shìqíng, xiào le hěn duō. Fēnkāi de shíhou tā shuō「bié tài jiǔ bù liánxì le」, wǒ shuō「hǎo」. Dàn hòulái wǒmen yòu hěn jiǔ méi liánxì le. Wǒ bǎ diànyǐng piào cóng kǒudài lǐ ná chūlái, jiā zài shū lǐ. Jiù wàitào kěyǐ rēng diào, dàn zhè zhāng piào wǒ xiǎng liú zhe. Tā bù zhǐ shì yì zhāng piào, tā shì yí gè zhèngjù — zhèngmíng nà tiān wǎnshang wǒmen quèshí hěn kāixīn.
text_en: While organizing the closet for the season change, I pulled out an old jacket. It was already a bit faded from washing, and in the right pocket I found a movie ticket. The date was winter four years ago. I can't remember the movie's name, but I remember that day. I watched it with an old friend I hadn't seen in ages. After graduating high school we went to different cities and rarely kept in touch. That winter he came on a business trip and asked to meet up. After the movie we went to a small restaurant, ordered beer and peanuts, and talked for over three hours. Nothing profound — just catching up on each other's lives, reminiscing about school days, laughing a lot. When parting he said 'don't go so long without being in touch,' and I said 'okay.' But then we went a long time without contact again. I took the ticket out of the pocket and pressed it in a book. The old jacket can be thrown away, but I want to keep this ticket. It's not just a ticket — it's evidence that we really were happy that night.

questions.q_zh: 旧外套口袋里有什么？
questions.q_en: What was in the old jacket's pocket?
questions.explanation: In the old jacket pocket was a movie ticket, its print already faded from going through the wash — a forgotten memento from the past.

questions.q_zh: 看完电影以后他们做了什么？
questions.q_en: What did they do after the movie?

options.pinyin: yòu kàn le yì chǎng diànyǐng

options.text: 去小饭馆喝酒聊了三个多小时
options.pinyin: qù xiǎo fànguǎn hē jiǔ liáo le sān gè duō xiǎoshí
options.text_en: Went to a small restaurant and chatted for over three hours

questions.explanation: After the movie, they went to a small restaurant and talked for over three hours -- unhurried reconnection.

title: The Bus at Dusk
text_zh: 每天傍晚坐公交车回家的时候，阳光刚好从车窗的一侧照进来。如果你坐在靠窗的位置，阳光会把你的脸晒得暖暖的。车里的人大多在看手机，但我喜欢看窗外。这个时间的天空特别好看，是那种橘红色渐渐变成紫色的颜色。有时候经过一座桥，能看到河面上反射着金色的光。我注意到每天差不多同一站上来的几个人：一个穿灰色外套的中年男人，总是靠着门站着闭眼休息；一个年轻女孩，耳朵里塞着耳机，嘴巴无声地跟着歌词在动；一个背着大书包的学生，上车就开始写作业。我不知道他们的名字，不知道他们的故事，但每天看到他们，会有一种奇怪的安心感。好像只要这些人还在，生活就是正常的，世界就是稳定的。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān bàngwǎn zuò gōngjiāo chē huí jiā de shíhou, yángguāng gānghǎo cóng chē chuāng de yí cè zhào jìnlái. Rúguǒ nǐ zuò zài kào chuāng de wèizhì, yángguāng huì bǎ nǐ de liǎn shài de nuǎn nuǎn de. Chē lǐ de rén dàduō zài kàn shǒujī, dàn wǒ xǐhuan kàn chuāng wài. Zhège shíjiān de tiānkōng tèbié hǎokàn, shì nà zhǒng jú hóngsè jiànjiàn biàn chéng zǐsè de yánsè. Yǒu shíhou jīngguò yí zuò qiáo, néng kàn dào hé miàn shàng fǎnshè zhe jīnsè de guāng. Wǒ zhùyì dào měi tiān chàbuduō tóng yí zhàn shàng lái de jǐ gè rén: yí gè chuān huīsè wàitào de zhōngnián nánrén, zǒng shì kào zhe mén zhàn zhe bì yǎn xiūxi; yí gè niánqīng nǚhái, ěrduo lǐ sāi zhe ěrjī, zuǐba wú shēng de gēn zhe gēcí zài dòng; yí gè bēi zhe dà shūbāo de xuéshēng, shàng chē jiù kāishǐ xiě zuòyè. Wǒ bù zhīdào tāmen de míngzi, bù zhīdào tāmen de gùshi, dàn měi tiān kàn dào tāmen, huì yǒu yì zhǒng qíguài de ānxīn gǎn. Hǎoxiàng zhǐyào zhèxiē rén hái zài, shēnghuó jiù shì zhèngcháng de, shìjiè jiù shì wěndìng de.
text_en: Every evening riding the bus home, sunlight shines through the windows from one side. If you sit by the window, the sun warms your face. Most people on the bus look at their phones, but I like looking outside. The sky at this hour is especially beautiful — that kind of orange-red gradually turning to purple. Sometimes crossing a bridge, you can see golden light reflected on the river. I've noticed a few people who board at roughly the same stop every day: a middle-aged man in a gray jacket who always stands by the door with eyes closed, resting; a young girl with earbuds in, lips silently mouthing song lyrics; a student with a big backpack who starts doing homework the moment they board. I don't know their names, don't know their stories, but seeing them every day brings a strange sense of reassurance. As if as long as these people are still here, life is normal and the world is stable.

questions.q_zh: 这段话让你想到了什么？
questions.q_en: What does this passage make you think of?
questions.explanation: The personal, sensory framing invites recall of the reader's own parallel moments.

questions.q_zh: 每天看到同样的人，作者有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How does the author feel seeing the same people every day?

options.pinyin: yǒu yì zhǒng qíguài de ānxīn gǎn

questions.explanation: Seeing the same unnamed faces daily creates a strange reassurance -- familiarity without obligation.

title: The Taste of Homesickness
text_zh: 来这个城市工作已经五年了，大部分时间我都不觉得想家。但有时候走在路上，突然闻到一股很熟悉的味道——也许是某家店在炖排骨汤，也许是巷子里飘出来的葱油饼的香味——我的鼻子就会比脑子先反应过来，心里一下子就软了。妈妈做的排骨汤是用砂锅慢慢炖的，要炖两个小时，汤是白色的，上面浮着一层薄薄的油。她总是先给我盛一碗最浓的。我每次回家都要喝两碗，她就在旁边看着我喝，脸上带着那种「吃得多我就放心了」的表情。上个周末我打电话给妈妈，她问我吃得怎么样，我说还行。其实那天我刚吃了一碗方便面。挂了电话以后我决定周末去超市买排骨，自己试着炖一锅汤。炖了两个小时打开锅盖，味道虽然不一样，但那股热气扑在脸上的感觉，让我觉得离家近了一点点。
text_pinyin: Lái zhège chéngshì gōngzuò yǐjīng wǔ nián le, dà bùfen shíjiān wǒ dōu bù juéde xiǎng jiā. Dàn yǒu shíhou zǒu zài lù shàng, tūrán wén dào yì gǔ hěn shúxī de wèidào — yěxǔ shì mǒu jiā diàn zài dùn páigǔ tāng, yěxǔ shì xiàngzi lǐ piāo chūlái de cōngyóu bǐng de xiāngwèi — wǒ de bízi jiù huì bǐ nǎozi xiān fǎnyìng guòlái, xīnlǐ yíxiàzi jiù ruǎn le. Māma zuò de páigǔ tāng shì yòng shāguō mànmàn dùn de, yào dùn liǎng gè xiǎoshí, tāng shì báisè de, shàngmiàn fú zhe yì céng báo báo de yóu. Tā zǒng shì xiān gěi wǒ chéng yì wǎn zuì nóng de. Wǒ měi cì huí jiā dōu yào hē liǎng wǎn, tā jiù zài pángbiān kàn zhe wǒ hē, liǎn shàng dài zhe nà zhǒng「chī de duō wǒ jiù fàngxīn le」de biǎoqíng. Shàng gè zhōumò wǒ dǎ diànhuà gěi māma, tā wèn wǒ chī de zěnmeyàng, wǒ shuō hái xíng. Qíshí nà tiān wǒ gāng chī le yì wǎn fāngbiàn miàn. Guà le diànhuà yǐhòu wǒ juédìng zhōumò qù chāoshì mǎi páigǔ, zìjǐ shì zhe dùn yì guō tāng. Dùn le liǎng gè xiǎoshí dǎkāi guō gài, wèidào suīrán bù yíyàng, dàn nà gǔ rè qì pū zài liǎn shàng de gǎnjué, ràng wǒ juéde lí jiā jìn le yìdiǎndiǎn.
text_en: I've been working in this city for five years, and most of the time I don't feel homesick. But sometimes walking down the street, I suddenly catch a familiar smell — maybe a shop stewing pork rib soup, maybe the aroma of scallion pancakes drifting from an alley — and my nose reacts before my brain does, my heart going soft all at once. Mom's pork rib soup was slowly stewed in a clay pot for two hours, the broth white with a thin layer of oil floating on top. She'd always serve me the richest bowl first. Every time I went home I'd drink two bowls while she watched, wearing that 'as long as you eat a lot I'm reassured' expression. Last weekend I called mom and she asked how I was eating. I said fine. Actually I'd just eaten instant noodles. After hanging up I decided to go to the supermarket that weekend and buy ribs to try stewing a pot of soup myself. After two hours I lifted the lid — the taste wasn't quite the same, but the hot steam hitting my face made me feel just a little closer to home.

questions.q_zh: 什么会让作者突然想家？
questions.q_en: What makes the author suddenly homesick?
questions.explanation: Homesickness hits through the nose: smelling a familiar scent triggers the feeling all at once.

questions.q_zh: 作者自己炖的汤和妈妈的一样吗？
questions.q_en: Was the author's soup the same as mom's?

options.text: 味道不一样，但热气的感觉让人觉得离家近了
options.pinyin: wèidào bù yíyàng, dàn rè qì de gǎnjué ràng rén juéde lí jiā jìn le
options.text_en: Taste was different, but the steam made it feel closer to home

options.pinyin: bǐ māma zuò de hái hǎo hē

questions.explanation: The soup tasted different from mom's, but the rising steam alone made home feel closer.

title: The Slow Season
text_zh: 秋天是一个慢下来的季节。树叶用很长的时间变颜色，从绿色慢慢变成黄色，再变成红色，最后安静地落在地上。不像春天那样急着发芽，也不像夏天那样热烈地生长。秋天好像在告诉我们：结束也可以是美丽的。我最喜欢秋天傍晚的光线，又柔又暖，把所有东西都染上了一层金色。走在银杏树下面，脚踩着厚厚的落叶，发出沙沙的声音。空气里有一种说不出来的香味，是落叶的味道，是果实成熟的味道，也是时间流过的味道。每年秋天我都会给自己放一天假，什么工作也不想，就去公园坐一个下午。看叶子落下来，看老人打太极，看小孩追着落叶跑。这些画面每年都一样，但每年看的时候心情都不太一样。
text_pinyin: Qiūtiān shì yí gè màn xiàlái de jìjié. Shùyè yòng hěn cháng de shíjiān biàn yánsè, cóng lǜsè mànmàn biàn chéng huángsè, zài biàn chéng hóngsè, zuìhòu ānjìng de luò zài dì shàng. Bú xiàng chūntiān nàyàng jí zhe fāyá, yě bú xiàng xiàtiān nàyàng rèliè de shēngzhǎng. Qiūtiān hǎoxiàng zài gàosu wǒmen: jiéshù yě kěyǐ shì měilì de. Wǒ zuì xǐhuan qiūtiān bàngwǎn de guāngxiàn, yòu róu yòu nuǎn, bǎ suǒyǒu dōngxi dōu rǎn shàng le yì céng jīnsè. Zǒu zài yínxìng shù xiàmiàn, jiǎo cǎi zhe hòu hòu de luòyè, fāchū shāshā de shēngyīn. Kōngqì lǐ yǒu yì zhǒng shuō bu chūlái de xiāngwèi, shì luòyè de wèidào, shì guǒshí chéngshú de wèidào, yě shì shíjiān liú guò de wèidào. Měi nián qiūtiān wǒ dōu huì gěi zìjǐ fàng yì tiān jià, shénme gōngzuò yě bù xiǎng, jiù qù gōngyuán zuò yí gè xiàwǔ. Kàn yèzi luò xiàlái, kàn lǎorén dǎ tàijí, kàn xiǎohái zhuī zhe luòyè pǎo. Zhèxiē huàmiàn měi nián dōu yíyàng, dàn měi nián kàn de shíhou xīnqíng dōu bú tài yíyàng. Yěxǔ zhè jiù shì jìjié cúnzài de yìyì — yòng tóngyàng de fēngjǐng tíxǐng nǐ, nǐ yǐjīng bú shì qùnián de nǐ le.
text_en: Autumn is a season that slows down. Leaves take a long time to change color, gradually from green to yellow, then to red, finally falling quietly to the ground. Not rushing to sprout like spring, nor growing fiercely like summer. Autumn seems to tell us: endings can be beautiful too. I love autumn's evening light best — soft and warm, coating everything in a layer of gold. Walking beneath ginkgo trees, feet crunching through thick fallen leaves with a rustling sound. The air carries an indescribable fragrance — the scent of fallen leaves, of ripe fruit, of time passing. Every autumn I give myself a day off, think nothing of work, and just sit in a park for an afternoon. Watch leaves fall, watch elders practice tai chi, watch children chase fallen leaves. These scenes are the same every year, but my mood while watching is always a little different.

questions.q_zh: 这段话想要读者从中获得什么？
questions.q_en: What does the passage want the reader to gain from it?

options.text: 一种新的看事物的方式
options.pinyin: yì zhǒng xīn de kàn shìwù de fāngshì
options.text_en: a new way of seeing things

questions.explanation: The passage reframes a familiar scene so the reader notices what they normally overlook.

questions.q_zh: 这段话传达的情绪是克制的还是外露的？
questions.q_en: Is the emotion conveyed in this passage restrained or overt?
questions.explanation: The passage embeds feeling in concrete detail rather than stating emotions outright.

title: The Repairman
text_zh: 小区门口有一个修鞋修包的摊位，是一位六十多岁的老师傅开的。他的摊位很小，只有一张桌子、一把椅子和一个装满工具的箱子。我的一双皮鞋开了胶，本来想扔掉买新的，但路过他的摊位的时候就停下来了。老师傅接过鞋看了看，说「能修，二十块钱」。他戴上老花镜，用一把小刷子仔细地把旧胶清理干净，涂上新胶，用夹子夹好，放在一边晾干。整个过程大概十五分钟，他做得很慢，但每一步都很仔细。等的时候我和他聊了几句，他说在这里摆了快二十年了。以前来修的人很多，现在越来越少了，大家都习惯坏了就买新的。他说「现在的东西也不如以前耐用了，修的意义确实没那么大了」。拿到修好的鞋以后，我觉得这双鞋好像比新鞋还让我珍惜。因为有人愿意花时间把它修好，而不是简单地把它扔掉。
text_pinyin: Xiǎoqū ménkǒu yǒu yí gè xiū xié xiū bāo de tānwèi, shì yí wèi liùshí duō suì de lǎo shīfu kāi de. Tā de tānwèi hěn xiǎo, zhǐ yǒu yì zhāng zhuōzi, yì bǎ yǐzi hé yí gè zhuāng mǎn gōngjù de xiāngzi. Wǒ de yì shuāng píxié kāi le jiāo, běnlái xiǎng rēng diào mǎi xīn de, dàn lùguò tā de tānwèi de shíhou jiù tíng xiàlái le. Lǎo shīfu jiē guò xié kàn le kàn, shuō「néng xiū, èrshí kuài qián」. Tā dài shàng lǎohuā jìng, yòng yì bǎ xiǎo shuāzi zǐxì de bǎ jiù jiāo qīnglǐ gānjìng, tú shàng xīn jiāo, yòng jiāzi jiā hǎo, fàng zài yìbiān liàng gān. Zhěnggè guòchéng dàgài shíwǔ fēnzhōng, tā zuò de hěn màn, dàn měi yí bù dōu hěn zǐxì. Děng de shíhou wǒ hé tā liáo le jǐ jù, tā shuō zài zhèlǐ bǎi le kuài èrshí nián le. Yǐqián lái xiū de rén hěn duō, xiànzài yuè lái yuè shǎo le, dàjiā dōu xíguàn huài le jiù mǎi xīn de. Tā shuō「xiànzài de dōngxi yě bù rú yǐqián nàiyòng le, xiū de yìyì quèshí méi nàme dà le」. Ná dào xiū hǎo de xié yǐhòu, wǒ juéde zhè shuāng xié hǎoxiàng bǐ xīn xié hái ràng wǒ zhēnxī. Yīnwèi yǒu rén yuànyì huā shíjiān bǎ tā xiū hǎo, ér bú shì jiǎndān de bǎ tā rēng diào.
text_en: Near the compound entrance there's a shoe and bag repair stall run by a craftsman in his sixties. His stall is small — just a table, a chair, and a toolbox. My leather shoes had come unglued and I was going to throw them out and buy new ones, but I stopped when passing his stall. The old master took the shoes, looked them over, and said 'fixable, twenty yuan.' He put on reading glasses, carefully cleaned off the old glue with a small brush, applied new glue, clamped them, and set them aside to dry. The whole process took about fifteen minutes — he worked slowly, but every step was careful. While waiting I chatted with him. He said he'd been here nearly twenty years. There used to be many customers; now fewer and fewer, since everyone is used to buying new when things break. He said 'things nowadays aren't as durable as before, so repairing really doesn't matter as much anymore.' After getting the repaired shoes back, I felt I treasured them more than new ones. Because someone was willing to spend time fixing them, rather than simply throwing them away.

questions.q_zh: 修鞋用了多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long did the shoe repair take?
questions.explanation: The old master worked slowly and carefully; the whole shoe repair took about fifteen minutes.

questions.q_zh: 老师傅为什么说来修东西的人越来越少了？
questions.q_en: Why does the old master say fewer people come for repairs?

options.text: 大家习惯坏了就买新的
options.pinyin: dàjiā xíguàn huài le jiù mǎi xīn de
options.text_en: Everyone is used to buying new when things break
The stall is too far away

questions.explanation: The passage explains: there used to be many customers.

title: Three in the Morning
text_zh: 凌晨三点醒来，发现窗外很安静。没有车的声音，没有人说话的声音，连虫子都不叫了。这种安静不是白天那种被噪音包围然后突然安静的感觉，而是一种真正的、完整的安静。我没有再去睡，而是起来倒了一杯水，坐在客厅的沙发上。黑暗中可以看到各种东西模糊的轮廓：茶几上的杯子，墙上的挂钟，地上那双拖鞋。白天的时候这些东西太普通了，不会注意到它们。但在凌晨三点，它们好像都变得安静而郑重，像是在认真地待在自己的位置上。我想起了一句话：「深夜是属于诚实的人的」。也许是因为在这个时间，所有的面具都摘下来了，所有不必要的想法都沉淀了，剩下的只有最真实的自己。喝完那杯水，我觉得心里平静了很多。然后回去躺下，很快就睡着了。
text_pinyin: Língchén sān diǎn xǐng lái, fāxiàn chuāng wài hěn ānjìng. Méiyǒu chē de shēngyīn, méiyǒu rén shuōhuà de shēngyīn, lián chóngzi dōu bú jiào le. Zhè zhǒng ānjìng bú shì báitiān nà zhǒng bèi zàoyīn bāowéi ránhòu tūrán ānjìng de gǎnjué, ér shì yì zhǒng zhēnzhèng de, wánzhěng de ānjìng. Wǒ méiyǒu zài qù shuì, ér shì qǐlái dào le yì bēi shuǐ, zuò zài kètīng de shāfā shàng. Hēi'àn zhōng kěyǐ kàn dào gè zhǒng dōngxi móhu de lúnkuò: chájī shàng de bēizi, qiáng shàng de guà zhōng, dì shàng nà shuāng tuōxié. Báitiān de shíhou zhèxiē dōngxi tài pǔtōng le, bú huì zhùyì dào tāmen. Dàn zài língchén sān diǎn, tāmen hǎoxiàng dōu biàn de ānjìng ér zhèngzhòng, xiàng shì zài rènzhēn de dāi zài zìjǐ de wèizhì shàng. Wǒ xiǎngqǐ le yí jù huà:「shēnyè shì shǔyú chéngshí de rén de」. Yěxǔ shì yīnwèi zài zhège shíjiān, suǒyǒu de miànjù dōu zhāi xiàlái le, suǒyǒu bú bìyào de xiǎngfǎ dōu chéndiàn le, shèng xià de zhǐ yǒu zuì zhēnshí de zìjǐ. Hē wán nà bēi shuǐ, wǒ juéde xīnlǐ píngjìng le hěn duō. Ránhòu huíqù tǎng xià, hěn kuài jiù shuì zháo le.
text_en: Woke at three in the morning to find it perfectly quiet outside. No cars, no voices, not even insects. This quiet isn't the daytime kind — noise surrounding you then suddenly stopping — but a true, complete quiet. I didn't go back to sleep. Instead I got up, poured a glass of water, and sat on the living room sofa. In the darkness I could see the vague outlines of things: the cup on the coffee table, the wall clock, the slippers on the floor. During the day these objects are too ordinary to notice. But at three in the morning they all seem quiet and solemn, as if earnestly staying in their places. I remembered a saying: 'The deep night belongs to honest people.' Maybe because at this hour all masks are removed, all unnecessary thoughts have settled, and what remains is only your most authentic self. After finishing that glass of water, my heart felt much calmer. Then I lay back down and fell asleep quickly.

questions.q_zh: 凌晨三点的安静和白天的安静有什么不同？
questions.q_en: How is the quiet at 3 AM different from daytime quiet?

options.text: 是一种真正的、完整的安静
options.pinyin: shì yì zhǒng zhēnzhèng de, wánzhěng de ānjìng

questions.explanation: The contrast is drawn explicitly: complete quiet.

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么觉得深夜属于诚实的人？
questions.q_en: Why does the author feel the deep night belongs to honest people?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi méiyǒu rén kàn dào nǐ

options.text: 因为所有面具都摘下来了，只剩最真实的自己
options.pinyin: yīnwèi suǒyǒu miànjù dōu zhāi xiàlái le, zhǐ shèng zuì zhēnshí de zìjǐ
options.text_en: Because all masks are removed, only the most authentic self remains
Because working at night is more efficient

questions.explanation: The passage explains: all unnecessary thoughts have settled.

title: The Library System
text_zh: 我常去的图书馆有一套安静但高效的系统。进门以后先刷身份证，门禁会发出很轻的一声「滴」。一楼是报纸和杂志区，灯光比较亮，可以说话但不能太大声。二楼是借阅区，按类别分好了，每排书架都有编号，找书很方便。三楼是自习区，绝对不能说话，连翻书的声音都尽量放轻。每层楼都有一个管理员，他们走路的时候鞋子不发出声音，像在水面上滑过一样。借书的流程也很简单：把书拿到一楼的自助机器上，扫一下身份证，再扫一下书的条形码，就可以带走了。还书也一样方便，门口有一个还书口，书塞进去就行。我特别喜欢这个图书馆的一个细节——每个座位旁边都有一个小灯，亮度刚好够看书，不会影响到旁边的人。这就是好系统的特点：每一个细节都是为了让人舒服。
text_pinyin: Wǒ cháng qù de túshūguǎn yǒu yí tào ānjìng dàn gāoxiào de xìtǒng. Jìn mén yǐhòu xiān shuā shēnfènzhèng, ménjìn huì fāchū hěn qīng de yì shēng「dī」. Yī lóu shì bàozhǐ hé zázhì qū, dēngguāng bǐjiào liàng, kěyǐ shuōhuà dàn bù néng tài dà shēng. Èr lóu shì jièyuè qū, àn lèibié fēn hǎo le, měi pái shūjià dōu yǒu biānhào, zhǎo shū hěn fāngbiàn. Sān lóu shì zìxí qū, juéduì bù néng shuōhuà, lián fān shū de shēngyīn dōu jǐnliàng fàng qīng. Měi céng lóu dōu yǒu yí gè guǎnlǐyuán, tāmen zǒulù de shíhou xiézi bù fāchū shēngyīn, xiàng zài shuǐ miàn shàng huá guò yíyàng. Jiè shū de liúchéng yě hěn jiǎndān: bǎ shū ná dào yī lóu de zìzhù jīqì shàng, sǎo yíxià shēnfènzhèng, zài sǎo yíxià shū de tiáoxíng mǎ, jiù kěyǐ dài zǒu le. Huán shū yě yíyàng fāngbiàn, ménkǒu yǒu yí gè huán shū kǒu, shū sāi jìnqù jiù xíng. Wǒ tèbié xǐhuan zhège túshūguǎn de yí gè xìjié — měi gè zuòwèi pángbiān dōu yǒu yí gè xiǎo dēng, liàngdù gānghǎo gòu kàn shū, bú huì yǐngxiǎng dào pángbiān de rén. Zhè jiù shì hǎo xìtǒng de tèdiǎn: měi yí gè xìjié dōu shì wèile ràng rén shūfu.
text_en: The library I often visit has a quiet but efficient system. Upon entering you swipe your ID card, the turnstile emitting a soft 'beep.' The first floor is the newspaper and magazine area — brighter lights, talking allowed but not too loud. The second floor is the borrowing area, organized by category, each shelf row numbered, making books easy to find. The third floor is the study area — absolutely no talking, even page-turning kept as quiet as possible. Each floor has an administrator who walks with shoes that make no sound, as if gliding over water. The borrowing process is simple: bring books to the first-floor self-service machine, scan your ID, scan the book's barcode, and take it home. Returns are equally convenient — a return slot by the entrance where you just slide books in. I especially love one detail about this library: every seat has a small lamp beside it, just bright enough to read by without disturbing the person next to you. That's the hallmark of a good system: every detail designed for comfort.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的核心主题是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the core theme of this passage?
questions.explanation: The passage's details all point toward people and their surroundings existing in quiet balance.

questions.q_zh: 作者特别喜欢图书馆的什么细节？
questions.q_en: What detail does the author especially like about the library?

options.text: 每个座位旁边有一个小灯
options.pinyin: měi gè zuòwèi pángbiān yǒu yí gè xiǎo dēng
options.text_en: A small lamp beside every seat

questions.explanation: The author's favorite detail is the small individual lamp beside every seat, giving each reader their own private circle of light.

title: The Hospital Waiting Room
text_zh: 去医院看病是一件需要耐心的事。挂号以后你会拿到一个号码，然后就开始等。候诊室的墙上有一个电子屏幕，显示现在叫到几号了。你可以算出前面大概还有多少人，每个人大概需要多长时间。我一般会带一本书，因为等两个小时是很正常的。候诊室的椅子是蓝色的塑料椅子，坐久了不太舒服。旁边的人在刷手机，有人在打电话，有小孩因为害怕哭了。护士穿着白色的衣服来来去去，走得很快，说话简洁有力。整个医院就像一台大机器，每个人都是其中的一个零件，各自做着自己的事情。虽然等待的过程很无聊，但我注意到了一件事：当屏幕上出现你的号码的时候，那一刻的感觉就像中了彩票一样高兴。
text_pinyin: Qù yīyuàn kànbìng shì yí jiàn xūyào nàixīn de shì. Guàhào yǐhòu nǐ huì ná dào yí gè hàomǎ, ránhòu jiù kāishǐ děng. Hòuzhěn shì de qiáng shàng yǒu yí gè diànzǐ píngmù, xiǎnshì xiànzài jiào dào jǐ hào le. Nǐ kěyǐ suàn chū qiánmiàn dàgài hái yǒu duōshǎo rén, měi gè rén dàgài xūyào duō cháng shíjiān. Wǒ yìbān huì dài yì běn shū, yīnwèi děng liǎng gè xiǎoshí shì hěn zhèngcháng de. Hòuzhěn shì de yǐzi shì lánsè de sùliào yǐzi, zuò jiǔ le bú tài shūfu. Pángbiān de rén zài shuā shǒujī, yǒu rén zài dǎ diànhuà, yǒu xiǎohái yīnwèi hàipà kū le. Hùshi chuān zhe báisè de yīfu lái lái qù qù, zǒu de hěn kuài, shuōhuà jiǎnjié yǒulì. Zhěnggè yīyuàn jiù xiàng yì tái dà jīqì, měi gè rén dōu shì qízhōng de yí gè língjiàn, gèzì zuò zhe zìjǐ de shìqíng. Suīrán děngdài de guòchéng hěn wúliáo, dàn wǒ zhùyì dào le yí jiàn shì: dāng píngmù shàng chūxiàn nǐ de hàomǎ de shíhou, nà yí kè de gǎnjué jiù xiàng zhòng le cǎipiào yíyàng gāoxìng. Yěxǔ děngdài běnshēn jiù shì yì zhǒng xùnliàn, xùnliàn nǐ xuéhuì zài shénme dōu bù néng zuò de shíhou bǎochí píngjìng.
text_en: Going to the hospital requires patience. After registering you get a number, then start waiting. The waiting room wall has an electronic screen showing the current number being called. You can estimate how many people are ahead and how long each takes. I usually bring a book since waiting two hours is normal. The waiting room has blue plastic chairs — uncomfortable after sitting long. People nearby scroll their phones, someone makes a call, a child cries from fear. Nurses in white come and go quickly, speaking concisely and firmly. The entire hospital is like a big machine, each person a component, doing their own job. Though waiting is boring, I noticed something: when your number appears on the screen, that moment feels as happy as winning the lottery.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的节奏感对阅读体验有什么影响？
questions.q_en: How does the pacing of this passage affect the reading experience?

options.pinyin: ràng dúzhě fàng màn sùdù qù gǎnshòu
options.text_en: slows the reader down to feel

options.pinyin: ràng dúzhě xiǎng kuài diǎn dú wán
options.text_en: makes the reader want to finish quickly

questions.q_zh: 这段话和说明文的写法有什么不同？
questions.q_en: How does this passage differ from an expository text?

options.text_en: focuses more on emotion and experience

title: The Neighborhood Committee
text_zh: 我们小区的居委会办公室在一楼，门上贴着办公时间和联系电话。里面只有三个工作人员，但管的事情很多：水电费的问题、邻居之间的纠纷、老人的关怀服务、垃圾分类的宣传……每天都有人来找他们。王阿姨是居委会的负责人，五十多岁，说话声音很大，但让人觉得很可靠。上周我家的水管坏了，我不知道该找谁来修。打电话给物业等了半天没人接。我走到居委会问了一下，王阿姨马上从抽屉里翻出一个本子，上面记满了各种维修师傅的电话号码。她帮我找到了一个水管工，还打电话帮我约好了时间。走的时候她说「修好了告诉我一声，要是没修好我再找别人」。这种基层的系统也许不那么高科技，但它有一种人情味。王阿姨的本子就是最好的数据库，而她的热情就是最好的算法。
text_pinyin: Wǒmen xiǎoqū de jūwěihuì bàngōngshì zài yī lóu, mén shàng tiē zhe bàngōng shíjiān hé liánxì diànhuà. Lǐmiàn zhǐ yǒu sān gè gōngzuò rényuán, dàn guǎn de shìqíng hěn duō: shuǐ diàn fèi de wèntí, línjū zhījiān de jiūfēn, lǎorén de guānhuái fúwù, lājī fēnlèi de xuānchuán……měi tiān dōu yǒu rén lái zhǎo tāmen. Wáng āyí shì jūwěihuì de fùzé rén, wǔshí duō suì, shuōhuà shēngyīn hěn dà, dàn ràng rén juéde hěn kěkào. Shàng zhōu wǒ jiā de shuǐguǎn huài le, wǒ bù zhīdào gāi zhǎo shéi lái xiū. Dǎ diànhuà gěi wùyè děng le bàntiān méi rén jiē. Wǒ zǒu dào jūwěihuì wèn le yíxià, Wáng āyí mǎshàng cóng chōuti lǐ fān chū yí gè běnzi, shàngmiàn jì mǎn le gè zhǒng wéixiū shīfu de diànhuà hàomǎ. Tā bāng wǒ zhǎo dào le yí gè shuǐguǎn gōng, hái dǎ diànhuà bāng wǒ yuē hǎo le shíjiān. Zǒu de shíhou tā shuō「xiū hǎo le gàosu wǒ yì shēng, yàoshi méi xiū hǎo wǒ zài zhǎo biéren」. Zhè zhǒng jīcéng de xìtǒng yěxǔ bù nàme gāo kējì, dàn tā yǒu yì zhǒng rénqíng wèi. Wáng āyí de běnzi jiù shì zuì hǎo de shùjùkù, ér tā de rèqíng jiù shì zuì hǎo de suànfǎ.
text_en: Our compound's neighborhood committee office is on the first floor, with office hours and phone numbers posted on the door. Only three staff members inside, but they manage many things: utility bill issues, neighbor disputes, elderly care services, recycling education — people come every day. Auntie Wang is the head, in her fifties, speaks loudly but feels very reliable. Last week my water pipe broke and I didn't know who to call. I phoned property management and waited a long time with no answer. I walked to the committee office and asked; Auntie Wang immediately pulled a notebook from her drawer, filled with phone numbers of various repair workers. She found me a plumber and even called to schedule a time for me. When I left she said 'let me know once it's fixed, and if it's not I'll find someone else.' This kind of grassroots system may not be high-tech, but it has human warmth. Auntie Wang's notebook is the best database, and her enthusiasm is the best algorithm.

questions.q_zh: 王阿姨怎么帮作者解决水管的问题？
questions.q_en: How did Auntie Wang help the author with the pipe problem?

options.pinyin: cóng běnzi lǐ zhǎo dào shuǐguǎn gōng de diànhuà bìng bāngmáng yuē shíjiān
options.text_en: Found a plumber's number from her notebook and scheduled a time
Told the author to call property management

questions.explanation: The understated tone and lingering final image leave the reader in a contemplative state.

title: The School Cafeteria
text_zh: 大学食堂是一个非常有趣的小社会。每天中午十一点半左右，所有人同时涌向食堂，像一条河流入大海。一楼是最便宜的窗口，菜的种类多但排队也最长。二楼有面条、饺子和麻辣烫，价格稍微贵一点。三楼是小炒窗口，可以现点现做，等的时间最长但味道最好。每个人都有自己的策略：有人十一点就去，避开人群；有人先去最远的窗口，因为那里排队的人少；有人每天固定吃一个窗口的饭，和打饭的阿姨已经很熟了，阿姨看到他就知道他要什么。我的策略是先看一圈哪个队最短就排哪个。四年下来，我发现食堂不只是吃饭的地方——你在那里认识了新朋友，准备了考试，讨论了人生的大问题，也独自吃过很多顿安静的午饭。毕业以后最怀念的不是哪道菜，而是那种排着队和朋友聊天的普通日子。
text_pinyin: Dàxué shítáng shì yí gè fēicháng yǒuqù de xiǎo shèhuì. Měi tiān zhōngwǔ shíyī diǎn bàn zuǒyòu, suǒyǒu rén tóngshí yǒng xiàng shítáng, xiàng yì tiáo hé liú rù dàhǎi. Yī lóu shì zuì piányi de chuāngkǒu, cài de zhǒnglèi duō dàn páiduì yě zuì cháng. Èr lóu yǒu miàntiáo, jiǎozi hé málà tàng, jiàgé shāowēi guì yìdiǎn. Sān lóu shì xiǎo chǎo chuāngkǒu, kěyǐ xiàn diǎn xiàn zuò, děng de shíjiān zuì cháng dàn wèidào zuì hǎo. Měi gè rén dōu yǒu zìjǐ de cèlüè: yǒu rén shíyī diǎn jiù qù, bìkāi rénqún; yǒu rén xiān qù zuì yuǎn de chuāngkǒu, yīnwèi nàlǐ páiduì de rén shǎo; yǒu rén měi tiān gùdìng chī yí gè chuāngkǒu de fàn, hé dǎ fàn de āyí yǐjīng hěn shú le, āyí kàn dào tā jiù zhīdào tā yào shénme. Wǒ de cèlüè shì xiān kàn yí quān nǎge duì zuì duǎn jiù pái nǎge. Sì nián xiàlái, wǒ fāxiàn shítáng bù zhǐ shì chīfàn de dìfāng — nǐ zài nàlǐ rènshi le xīn péngyou, zhǔnbèi le kǎoshì, tǎolùn le rénshēng de dà wèntí, yě dúzì chī guò hěn duō dùn ānjìng de wǔfàn. Bìyè yǐhòu zuì huáiniàn de bú shì nǎ dào cài, ér shì nà zhǒng pái zhe duì hé péngyou liáo tiān de pǔtōng rìzi.
text_en: A university cafeteria is a fascinating small society. Every day around 11:30, everyone surges toward the cafeteria like a river flowing into the sea. The first floor has the cheapest windows — many dishes but the longest lines. The second floor has noodles, dumplings, and malatang, slightly pricier. The third floor is the stir-fry counter where dishes are made to order — longest wait but best taste. Everyone has their strategy: some go at eleven to avoid crowds; some head to the farthest window since fewer people line up there; some eat at the same counter every day, so familiar with the serving auntie that she knows their order on sight. My strategy was to scan which line was shortest and join that one. Over four years, I found the cafeteria was more than a place to eat — you made new friends there, studied for exams, discussed life's big questions, and also ate many quiet lunches alone. After graduation what I miss most isn't any particular dish, but those ordinary days of chatting with friends while standing in line.

questions.q_zh: 三楼的窗口有什么特点？
questions.q_en: What's special about the third floor counter?

options.text: 可以现点现做，味道最好但等最久
options.pinyin: kěyǐ xiàn diǎn xiàn zuò, wèidào zuì hǎo dàn děng zuì jiǔ
options.text_en: Made to order, best taste but longest wait

questions.explanation: The third-floor counter makes food to order, so the wait is longest but the taste is best — a direct tradeoff between patience and quality.

questions.q_zh: 这段话暗示了什么情感？
questions.q_en: What emotion does this passage hint at?
questions.explanation: Beneath the surface details runs an undercurrent of something passing or already gone.

title: The Express Delivery Network
text_zh: 中国的快递系统可能是世界上最高效的之一。我在网上买了一件衣服，从广州发出来，三天就到了北京。包裹从卖家的仓库开始，先送到广州的分拣中心，在那里被扫描、分类，然后装上卡车。到了中转站又被重新分拣，送上了去北京的货车。到北京以后先去大的分拣中心，再送到我家附近的快递站。快递员骑着电动三轮车把包裹送到我的小区，如果我不在家就放在快递柜里。整个过程中间经过了至少六七个环节，每个环节都有人在工作，但我只需要在手机上点一下「确认收货」就行了。我的快递员姓李，三十多岁，每天送两三百个包裹。他总是很忙，但看到我会说一句「今天有您的包裹」。有一次下大雨他还是准时送到了，包裹外面裹了一层塑料袋防水。这种细节没有人要求他做，但他做了。系统的背后是一个个具体的人。
text_pinyin: Zhōngguó de kuàidì xìtǒng kěnéng shì shìjiè shàng zuì gāoxiào de zhī yī. Wǒ zài wǎng shàng mǎi le yí jiàn yīfu, cóng Guǎngzhōu fā chūlái, sān tiān jiù dào le Běijīng. Bāoguǒ cóng màijiā de cāngkù kāishǐ, xiān sòng dào Guǎngzhōu de fēnjiǎn zhōngxīn, zài nàlǐ bèi sǎomiáo, fēnlèi, ránhòu zhuāng shàng kǎchē. Dào le zhōngzhuǎn zhàn yòu bèi chóngxīn fēnjiǎn, sòng shàng le qù Běijīng de huòchē. Dào Běijīng yǐhòu xiān qù dà de fēnjiǎn zhōngxīn, zài sòng dào wǒ jiā fùjìn de kuàidì zhàn. Kuàidì yuán qí zhe diàndòng sānlún chē bǎ bāoguǒ sòng dào wǒ de xiǎoqū, rúguǒ wǒ bú zài jiā jiù fàng zài kuàidì guì lǐ. Zhěnggè guòchéng zhōngjiān jīngguò le zhìshǎo liù qī gè huánjié, měi gè huánjié dōu yǒu rén zài gōngzuò, dàn wǒ zhǐ xūyào zài shǒujī shàng diǎn yíxià「quèrèn shōuhuò」jiù xíng le. Wǒ de kuàidì yuán xìng Lǐ, sānshí duō suì, měi tiān sòng liǎng sān bǎi gè bāoguǒ. Tā zǒng shì hěn máng, dàn kàn dào wǒ huì shuō yí jù「jīntiān yǒu nín de bāoguǒ」. Yǒu yí cì xià dà yǔ tā háishi zhǔnshí sòng dào le, bāoguǒ wàimiàn guǒ le yì céng sùliào dài fángshuǐ. Zhè zhǒng xìjié méiyǒu rén yāoqiú tā zuò, dàn tā zuò le. Xìtǒng de bèihòu shì yí gè gè jùtǐ de rén.
text_en: China's delivery system is probably one of the most efficient in the world. I bought a piece of clothing online, shipped from Guangzhou, and it arrived in Beijing in three days. The package starts at the seller's warehouse, goes to a Guangzhou sorting center where it's scanned and categorized, then loaded onto a truck. At a transit station it's re-sorted and put on a truck to Beijing. In Beijing it goes to a large sorting center, then to the delivery station near my home. The courier rides an electric tricycle to deliver packages to my compound; if I'm not home, it goes in the delivery locker. The whole process passes through at least six or seven stages, each with people working, but I only need to tap 'confirm receipt' on my phone. My courier is surnamed Li, in his thirties, delivering two to three hundred packages daily. He's always busy, but when he sees me he says 'got a package for you today.' Once during heavy rain he still delivered on time, the package wrapped in an extra plastic bag for waterproofing. Nobody required this detail, but he did it. Behind every system are individual people.

questions.q_zh: 作者想通过这个故事探讨什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author want to explore through this story?

options.text: 日常生活中的深层意义
options.pinyin: rìcháng shēnghuó zhōng de shēncéng yìyì

questions.explanation: The ordinary scenario serves as a vehicle for uncovering overlooked significance in routines.

questions.q_zh: 下雨天快递员做了什么特别的事？
questions.q_en: What special thing did the courier do on the rainy day?

options.text: 用塑料袋把包裹包起来防水
options.pinyin: yòng sùliào dài bǎ bāoguǒ bāo qǐlái fángshuǐ
options.text_en: Wrapped the package in plastic for waterproofing

options.pinyin: dǎ diànhuà ràng zuòzhě qù qǔ
options.text_en: Called the author to pick it up

questions.explanation: On the rainy day, the courier wrapped the package in plastic before delivering -- care beyond the job description.

title: The Subway Ecosystem
text_zh: 每天坐地铁上下班的人形成了一种独特的生态系统。早上七点到九点是高峰期，车厢里挤得几乎无法动弹。每个人都有一套自己的生存策略：有人总是站在车门旁边的角落里，靠着墙不容易被挤；有人知道哪一节车厢人最少，每次都走到那个位置等车；有人已经算好了到站以后上楼梯最快的车门是哪个。车厢里虽然很挤，但大家都保持着一种默契——背包背在前面，不占太多空间；手机声音关掉或者戴耳机；到站了要下车的时候说一声「让一让」大家就会让出一条路。地铁还有一种特殊的时间感。因为在地下，没有天空可以看，也不知道外面是晴天还是下雨。你只能通过站名来判断位置，通过播报来感知时间。每天在同样的时间、同样的线路、同样的车厢里，日子就这么一站一站地过去了。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zuò dìtiě shàng xià bān de rén xíngchéng le yì zhǒng dútè de shēngtài xìtǒng. Zǎoshang qī diǎn dào jiǔ diǎn shì gāofēng qī, chēxiāng lǐ jǐ de jīhū wúfǎ dòngtán. Měi gè rén dōu yǒu yí tào zìjǐ de shēngcún cèlüè: yǒu rén zǒng shì zhàn zài chē mén pángbiān de jiǎoluò lǐ, kào zhe qiáng bù róngyì bèi jǐ; yǒu rén zhīdào nǎ yì jié chēxiāng rén zuì shǎo, měi cì dōu zǒu dào nàge wèizhì děng chē; yǒu rén yǐjīng suàn hǎo le dào zhàn yǐhòu shàng lóutī zuì kuài de chē mén shì nǎge. Chēxiāng lǐ suīrán hěn jǐ, dàn dàjiā dōu bǎochí zhe yì zhǒng mòqì — bēibāo bēi zài qiánmiàn, bù zhàn tài duō kōngjiān; shǒujī shēngyīn guān diào huòzhě dài ěrjī; dào zhàn le yào xià chē de shíhou shuō yì shēng「ràng yí ràng」dàjiā jiù huì ràng chū yì tiáo lù. Dìtiě hái yǒu yì zhǒng tèshū de shíjiān gǎn. Yīnwèi zài dìxià, méiyǒu tiānkōng kěyǐ kàn, yě bù zhīdào wàimiàn shì qíngtiān háishi xià yǔ. Nǐ zhǐ néng tōngguò zhàn míng lái pànduàn wèizhì, tōngguò bōbào lái gǎnzhī shíjiān. Měi tiān zài tóngyàng de shíjiān, tóngyàng de xiànlù, tóngyàng de chēxiāng lǐ, rìzi jiù zhème yí zhàn yí zhàn de guòqù le.
text_en: People who commute by subway daily form a unique ecosystem. Between 7 and 9 AM is rush hour — cars so packed you can barely move. Everyone has survival strategies: some always stand in the corner by the door, leaning against the wall to avoid being squeezed; some know which car has fewest people and always walk to that spot; some have calculated which door leads to the fastest stairway exit. Though the cars are crowded, everyone maintains an unspoken agreement — backpacks worn in front to save space, phone sounds off or earbuds in, and when someone needs to exit they say 'excuse me' and a path opens up. The subway also has a peculiar sense of time. Being underground, there's no sky to see, no way to know if it's sunny or raining outside. You can only judge location by station names and sense time through announcements. Every day at the same time, same line, same car — days pass station by station.

questions.q_zh: 高峰期车厢里大家有什么默契？
questions.q_en: What unspoken agreements do people keep during rush hour?

options.text: 背包背在前面，手机静音
options.pinyin: bēibāo bēi zài qiánmiàn, shǒujī jìngyīn
měi gè rén zhàn zài gùdìng wèizhì
options.text_en: Everyone stands in a fixed spot

questions.explanation: Rush-hour convention: backpacks moved to the front, phones kept silent -- unspoken rules everyone follows.

questions.q_zh: 地铁里的时间感有什么特别？
questions.q_en: What's special about the sense of time in the subway?

options.text: 看不到天空，只能通过站名和播报感知时间
options.pinyin: kàn bú dào tiānkōng, zhǐ néng tōngguò zhàn míng hé bōbào gǎnzhī shíjiān
options.text_en: Can't see the sky, can only sense time through station names and announcements

options.text: 每一站停的时间都不一样
options.pinyin: měi yí zhàn tíng de shíjiān dōu bù yíyàng
options.text_en: Each stop lasts a different time
There's a large clock to check

questions.explanation: Underground with no sky visible, passengers can only track where they are through station names and announcements — normal time cues like sunlight are absent.

title: The Night Shift
text_zh: 便利店的夜班从晚上十一点开始，到早上七点结束。我的朋友小陈在一家便利店做兼职夜班已经两年了。他告诉我，夜班有自己的节奏。十一点到十二点是最忙的时候，下班的人会来买零食和啤酒。十二点以后人就少了，偶尔有人来买烟或者泡面。凌晨两三点是最安静的时候，有时候一个小时都没有客人。这个时候小陈要做的事情是：检查商品的保质期、整理货架、擦地、补货。他说最喜欢补货的过程，把一箱箱的东西拆开，按照顺序整整齐齐地摆好，有一种说不出来的满足感。凌晨四点以后开始有上早班的人来买早餐和咖啡。六点左右环卫工人会进来买热水或者包子。小陈说他已经认识了很多夜班的常客，虽然大家很少说话，但彼此点个头就够了。城市在白天是属于所有人的，但在夜里，它属于这些安静地维持着运转的人。
text_pinyin: Biànlì diàn de yèbān cóng wǎnshang shíyī diǎn kāishǐ, dào zǎoshang qī diǎn jiéshù. Wǒ de péngyou Xiǎo Chén zài yì jiā biànlì diàn zuò jiānzhí yèbān yǐjīng liǎng nián le. Tā gàosu wǒ, yèbān yǒu zìjǐ de jiézòu. Shíyī diǎn dào shí'èr diǎn shì zuì máng de shíhou, xiàbān de rén huì lái mǎi língshí hé píjiǔ. Shí'èr diǎn yǐhòu rén jiù shǎo le, ǒu'ěr yǒu rén lái mǎi yān huòzhě pàomiàn. Língchén liǎng sān diǎn shì zuì ānjìng de shíhou, yǒu shíhou yí gè xiǎoshí dōu méiyǒu kèrén. Zhège shíhou Xiǎo Chén yào zuò de shìqíng shì: jiǎnchá shāngpǐn de bǎozhìqī, zhěnglǐ huòjià, cā dì, bǔ huò. Tā shuō zuì xǐhuan bǔ huò de guòchéng, bǎ yì xiāng xiāng de dōngxi chāi kāi, ànzhào shùnxù zhěngzhěng qíqí de bǎi hǎo, yǒu yì zhǒng shuō bu chūlái de mǎnzú gǎn. Língchén sì diǎn yǐhòu kāishǐ yǒu shàng zǎobān de rén lái mǎi zǎocān hé kāfēi. Liù diǎn zuǒyòu huánwèi gōngrén huì jìnlái mǎi rè shuǐ huòzhě bāozi. Xiǎo Chén shuō tā yǐjīng rènshi le hěn duō yèbān de chángkè, suīrán dàjiā hěn shǎo shuōhuà, dàn bǐcǐ diǎn gè tóu jiù gòu le. Chéngshì zài báitiān shì shǔyú suǒyǒu rén de, dàn zài yèlǐ, tā shǔyú zhèxiē ānjìng de wéichí zhe yùnzhuǎn de rén.
text_en: The convenience store night shift starts at 11 PM and ends at 7 AM. My friend Xiao Chen has been working part-time night shifts at a convenience store for two years. He told me night shifts have their own rhythm. 11 PM to midnight is busiest — people getting off work buy snacks and beer. After midnight it's quieter, the occasional person buying cigarettes or instant noodles. 2-3 AM is the quietest — sometimes an hour without a single customer. During this time Xiao Chen checks expiration dates, organizes shelves, mops the floor, and restocks. He says he likes restocking best — opening boxes one by one, arranging items neatly in order, with an indescribable sense of satisfaction. After 4 AM, early shift workers start coming for breakfast and coffee. Around 6, sanitation workers come in for hot water or steamed buns. Xiao Chen says he's come to know many night-shift regulars. Though they rarely talk, a nod to each other is enough. The city during the day belongs to everyone, but at night it belongs to these people who quietly keep things running.

questions.q_zh: 小陈最喜欢夜班的哪个工作？
questions.q_en: What's Xiao Chen's favorite night shift task?
questions.explanation: Xiao Chen's favorite night shift task is restocking shelves — the quiet, methodical work of organizing products when the store is nearly empty.

questions.q_zh: 凌晨六点左右谁会来便利店？
questions.q_en: Who comes to the convenience store around 6 AM?
questions.explanation: The passage identifies: around 6, sanitation workers come in for hot.

title: The Exam Hall
text_zh: 考试那天，所有人提前半小时到了考场门口。每个人手里拿着准考证和身份证，表情各不相同。有人在低声背单词，有人闭着眼睛深呼吸，有人在和旁边的人小声讨论某个知识点。考场的门准时打开，监考老师站在门口检查证件。进去以后，每张桌子上已经放好了答题卡和草稿纸。桌子之间的距离刚好够大，让你看不到别人的卷子。铃声响了以后，整个教室突然安静下来，只有翻卷子的声音和笔在纸上写字的声音。时间一分一秒地过去，你能感觉到空气中的紧张感。中间有人上厕所，监考老师陪着去，每一步都在制度的规定之内。考试结束的铃声响起来的时候，有人长出了一口气，有人脸色发白。走出考场，外面的阳光让人觉得恍惚，好像刚从另一个世界回来。但无论结果怎样，这一切都结束了，这本身就是一种解脱。
text_pinyin: Kǎoshì nà tiān, suǒyǒu rén tíqián bàn xiǎoshí dào le kǎochǎng ménkǒu. Měi gè rén shǒu lǐ ná zhe zhǔn kǎo zhèng hé shēnfènzhèng, biǎoqíng gè bù xiāngtóng. Yǒu rén zài dī shēng bèi dāncí, yǒu rén bì zhe yǎnjīng shēn hūxī, yǒu rén zài hé pángbiān de rén xiǎo shēng tǎolùn mǒu gè zhīshi diǎn. Kǎochǎng de mén zhǔnshí dǎkāi, jiānkǎo lǎoshī zhàn zài ménkǒu jiǎnchá zhèngjiàn. Jìnqù yǐhòu, měi zhāng zhuōzi shàng yǐjīng fàng hǎo le dátí kǎ hé cǎogǎo zhǐ. Zhuōzi zhījiān de jùlí gānghǎo gòu dà, ràng nǐ kàn bú dào biéren de juǎnzi. Líng shēng xiǎng le yǐhòu, zhěnggè jiàoshì tūrán ānjìng xiàlái, zhǐ yǒu fān juǎnzi de shēngyīn hé bǐ zài zhǐ shàng xiě zì de shēngyīn. Shíjiān yì fēn yì miǎo de guòqù, nǐ néng gǎnjué dào kōngqì zhōng de jǐnzhāng gǎn. Zhōngjiān yǒu rén shàng cèsuǒ, jiānkǎo lǎoshī péi zhe qù, měi yí bù dōu zài zhìdù de guīdìng zhī nèi. Kǎoshì jiéshù de líng shēng xiǎng qǐlái de shíhou, yǒu rén cháng chū le yì kǒu qì, yǒu rén liǎnsè fā bái. Zǒu chū kǎochǎng, wàimiàn de yángguāng ràng rén juéde huǎnghū, hǎoxiàng gāng cóng lìng yí gè shìjiè huílái. Dàn wúlùn jiéguǒ zěnyàng, zhè yíqiè dōu jiéshù le, zhè běnshēn jiù shì yì zhǒng jiětuō.
text_en: On exam day, everyone arrived half an hour early at the exam hall entrance. Each person held their admission ticket and ID, expressions all different. Some murmured vocabulary, some breathed deeply with eyes closed, some quietly discussed a topic with someone nearby. The hall doors opened on time, the proctor standing at the entrance checking documents. Inside, each desk already had an answer sheet and scratch paper. The distance between desks was just enough that you couldn't see others' papers. After the bell, the entire classroom fell suddenly silent — only the sound of turning pages and pens writing on paper. Time passed second by second; you could feel the tension in the air. When someone needed the restroom, the proctor accompanied them — every step within the rules. When the ending bell rang, some exhaled deeply, some went pale. Walking out, the sunlight outside felt dizzying, as if returning from another world. But regardless of the result, it was all over, and that itself was a kind of relief.

questions.q_zh: 铃声响了以后教室里有什么声音？
questions.q_en: What sounds were in the classroom after the bell?

options.pinyin: fān juǎnzi hé xiě zì de shēngyīn

questions.explanation: After the bell, the room fills with turning pages and the scratch of writing -- focused, collective effort.

questions.q_zh: 走出考场以后作者什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How did the author feel after leaving the exam hall?

options.text: 觉得恍惚，好像从另一个世界回来
options.pinyin: juéde huǎnghū, hǎoxiàng cóng lìng yí gè shìjiè huílái
options.text_en: Felt dazed, as if returning from another world

options.pinyin: mǎshàng xiǎng kǎo xià yì chǎng
options.text_en: Immediately wanted to take the next exam

questions.explanation: Walking out of the exam hall, the author felt dazed, as if returning from a separate world.

title: The Post Office
text_zh: 我家附近的邮局是一栋绿色的老房子，已经有三十多年了。里面的装修很简单，地上铺着白色的瓷砖，墙上挂着价格表和寄件须知。柜台后面坐着两个工作人员，一个负责寄信和包裹，另一个负责汇款和其他业务。现在来邮局的人比以前少多了。以前过年的时候这里排队排到门外面，大家都来寄年货和明信片。现在大部分人都用快递和电子转账了，邮局变得安静了。但是有一些事情还是只有邮局能做：寄挂号信、办理邮政储蓄、买邮票。上周我来寄一封挂号信，工作人员很仔细地帮我称了重量，贴了邮票，在信封上盖了邮戳。那个邮戳的声音——「咔嗒」一下——让我突然觉得很踏实。在一个什么都变快的世界里，邮局还在用它自己的速度做事。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā fùjìn de yóujú shì yí dòng lǜsè de lǎo fángzi, yǐjīng yǒu sānshí duō nián le. Lǐmiàn de zhuāngxiū hěn jiǎndān, dì shàng pū zhe báisè de cízhuān, qiáng shàng guà zhe jiàgé biǎo hé jì jiàn xūzhī. Guìtái hòumiàn zuò zhe liǎng gè gōngzuò rényuán, yí gè fùzé jì xìn hé bāoguǒ, lìng yí gè fùzé huìkuǎn hé qítā yèwù. Xiànzài lái yóujú de rén bǐ yǐqián shǎo duō le. Yǐqián guò nián de shíhou zhèlǐ páiduì pái dào mén wàimiàn, dàjiā dōu lái jì niánhuò hé míngxìnpiàn. Xiànzài dà bùfen rén dōu yòng kuàidì hé diànzǐ zhuǎnzhàng le, yóujú biàn de ānjìng le. Dànshì yǒu yìxiē shìqíng háishi zhǐ yǒu yóujú néng zuò: jì guàhào xìn, bànlǐ yóuzhèng chǔxù, mǎi yóupiào. Shàng zhōu wǒ lái jì yì fēng guàhào xìn, gōngzuò rényuán hěn zǐxì de bāng wǒ chēng le zhòngliàng, tiē le yóupiào, zài xìnfēng shàng gài le yóuchuō. Nàge yóuchuō de shēngyīn —— 「kādā」yíxià —— ràng wǒ tūrán juéde hěn tāshi. Zài yí gè shénme dōu biàn kuài de shìjiè lǐ, yóujú hái zài yòng tā zìjǐ de sùdù zuò shì. Yěxǔ bú shì suǒyǒu shìqíng dōu xūyào kuài, yǒuxiē shìqíng zhíde mànmàn lái.
text_en: The post office near my home is an old green building, over thirty years old. The interior is simple — white floor tiles, a price list and mailing guidelines on the wall. Behind the counter sit two workers, one handling letters and packages, the other money transfers and other services. Far fewer people come to the post office now. During New Year's it used to have lines stretching outside, everyone mailing New Year goods and postcards. Now most people use express delivery and electronic transfers, so the post office has become quiet. But some things only the post office can do: sending registered mail, postal savings, buying stamps. Last week I came to send a registered letter. The worker carefully weighed it, affixed stamps, and stamped the postmark on the envelope. That postmark sound — 'click' — suddenly made me feel grounded. In a world where everything gets faster, the post office still works at its own pace.

questions.q_zh: 现在邮局为什么比以前安静了？
questions.q_en: Why is the post office quieter now than before?

options.text: 大部分人用快递和电子转账了
options.pinyin: dà bùfen rén yòng kuàidì hé diànzǐ zhuǎnzhàng le
options.text_en: Most people use express delivery and electronic transfers

questions.explanation: The passage explains: so the post office has become quiet.

questions.q_zh: 邮戳的声音让作者有什么感觉？
questions.q_en: How did the postmark sound make the author feel?
questions.explanation: The solid thump of the postmark stamp gave the author a grounded, reassured feeling.

title: The Teacher's Desk
text_zh: 我上高中的时候，班主任李老师的办公桌总是堆满了东西。试卷、作业本、参考书一摞一摞地堆着，但是她总能在几秒钟之内找到需要的东西。她的桌子虽然看起来很乱，但其实有自己的系统。左边一摞是还没批改的作业，右边是已经批改好的。中间放着一个红色的笔筒，里面插满了红色的笔——她说红笔是老师最重要的工具。桌子角上有一个小台灯和一杯茶，茶早就凉了，因为她每次倒完茶就忘了喝。每天放学以后，总有学生来找她问问题。她会把椅子拉出来让学生坐，自己站着讲。讲完以后她会问「听懂了吗？」如果学生说听懂了，她会再出一道类似的题让他做，确认真的懂了才放人走。二十年过去了，我还记得那张堆满东西的桌子，还记得那杯永远凉着的茶。有些系统不在电脑里，在一个人的习惯里。
text_pinyin: Wǒ shàng gāozhōng de shíhou, bānzhǔrèn Lǐ lǎoshī de bàngōngzhuō zǒng shì duī mǎn le dōngxi. Shìjuàn, zuòyè běn, cānkǎo shū yì luò yì luò de duī zhe, dànshì tā zǒng néng zài jǐ miǎozhōng zhī nèi zhǎo dào xūyào de dōngxi. Tā de zhuōzi suīrán kàn qǐlái hěn luàn, dàn qíshí yǒu zìjǐ de xìtǒng. Zuǒbiān yì luò shì hái méi pīgǎi de zuòyè, yòubiān shì yǐjīng pīgǎi hǎo de. Zhōngjiān fàng zhe yí gè hóngsè de bǐtǒng, lǐmiàn chā mǎn le hóngsè de bǐ — tā shuō hóng bǐ shì lǎoshī zuì zhòngyào de gōngjù. Zhuōzi jiǎo shàng yǒu yí gè xiǎo táidēng hé yì bēi chá, chá zǎo jiù liáng le, yīnwèi tā měi cì dào wán chá jiù wàng le hē. Měi tiān fàngxué yǐhòu, zǒng yǒu xuéshēng lái zhǎo tā wèn wèntí. Tā huì bǎ yǐzi lā chūlái ràng xuéshēng zuò, zìjǐ zhàn zhe jiǎng. Jiǎng wán yǐhòu tā huì wèn「tīng dǒng le ma?」Rúguǒ xuéshēng shuō tīng dǒng le, tā huì zài chū yí dào lèisì de tí ràng tā zuò, quèrèn zhēn de dǒng le cái fàng rén zǒu. Èrshí nián guòqù le, wǒ hái jìde nà zhāng duī mǎn dōngxi de zhuōzi, hái jìde nà bēi yǒngyuǎn liáng zhe de chá. Yǒuxiē xìtǒng bú zài diànnǎo lǐ, zài yí gè rén de xíguàn lǐ.
text_en: When I was in high school, our homeroom teacher Ms. Li's desk was always piled with things. Test papers, homework books, reference books stacked in piles, yet she could always find what she needed within seconds. Though her desk looked messy, it actually had its own system. The left pile was ungraded homework, the right was already graded. In the middle sat a red pen holder full of red pens — she said red pens are a teacher's most important tool. On the desk corner was a small lamp and a cup of tea, the tea long gone cold because she always forgot to drink after pouring. After school every day, students would come to ask questions. She'd pull out her chair for the student to sit while she stood to explain. After explaining she'd ask 'do you understand?' If the student said yes, she'd give them a similar problem to solve, only letting them leave after confirming they truly understood. Twenty years have passed and I still remember that desk piled with things, still remember that forever-cold cup of tea. Some systems don't exist in computers — they exist in a person's habits.

questions.q_zh: 李老师桌子上的系统是什么？
questions.q_en: What was the system on Ms. Li's desk?

options.text: 左边没批改的，右边批改好的
options.pinyin: zuǒbiān méi pīgǎi de, yòubiān pīgǎi hǎo de
options.text_en: Left side ungraded, right side graded

options.pinyin: dōu yòng wénjiàn jiā zhuāng hǎo

questions.explanation: Her desk was split into two clear zones: ungraded papers on the left, finished ones on the right — a simple spatial system for tracking progress.

questions.q_zh: 学生说听懂了以后李老师会怎么做？
questions.q_en: What would Ms. Li do after a student said they understood?

options.text: 再出一道类似的题确认真的懂了
options.pinyin: zài chū yí dào lèisì de tí quèrèn zhēn de dǒng le
options.text_en: Give a similar problem to confirm understanding
